6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechHigherDirectImage
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FreePresheafComplex
10	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechAcyclic
11	
12	/-!
13	# Čech bridge — assembly layer
14	
15	This file is the downstream assembly layer that consumes both:
16	
17	- `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplex` (from `PresheafCech.lean`): the section Čech
18	  cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F) : CochainComplex Ab ℕ` built cosimplicially from
19	  `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`.
20	
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex` (from `FreePresheafComplex.lean`): the
22	  free-presheaf chain complex `K(𝒰)_• : ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose
23	  degree-`p` term is `∐_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`.
24	
25	The reason this file must sit downstream of both is that `FreePresheafComplex.lean`
26	already imports `PresheafCech.lean`, so any file needing both must import
27	`FreePresheafComplex.lean` (which transitively brings in `PresheafCech.lean`).
28	
29	## Declarations
30	
31	- `cechComplex_hom_identification` (**proved**, line ~241): the per-degree `Ab`-isomorphism
32	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)` intertwining the differentials, assembled cosimplicially
33	  via `homCechSectionCosimplicialIso` and `(alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).mapIso`.
34	
35	- `homCechComplexMapOpIso` / `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso` (**proved**, project-local Mathlib
36	  supplement below): the contravariant-transport bridge. `homCechComplexMapOpIso` identifies
37	  the alternating-coface hom-complex `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` with the mapped opposite
38	  `Hom(-, F)((K(𝒰)_•)ᵒᵖ)` of the free Čech complex (same degreewise terms; the differential
39	  squares are the degreewise identity `homCechComplex_d_eq`).  `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso`
40	  composes it with `cechComplex_hom_identification` to identify `Č•(coverOpen 𝒰, F)` with the
41	  mapped opposite of the free complex.  This is the precise bridge that lets
42	  `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` turn Lane-1's free resolution into Čech
43	  acyclicity in a single step.
44	
45	- `preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective` /
46	  `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (**proved**, project-local Mathlib
47	  supplement below): the categorical bridge step. For an injective object `I` of an abelian
48	  category, the contravariant `Hom(-, I) = preadditiveYoneda.obj I` is exact (preserves
49	  finite colimits, hence homology), so it carries a quasi-isomorphism of chain complexes to
50	  a quasi-isomorphism of the `Hom`-cochain complex. This is the ingredient that turns the
51	  free-complex resolution `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` (Lane 1) and
52	  `cechComplex_hom_identification` into the positive-degree vanishing below.
53	
54	- `injective_cech_acyclic` (**proved**, project-local Mathlib supplement below): Čech
55	  cohomology vanishes in positive degrees for injective sheaves.  Built as the one-step
56	  op-transport assembly of `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (Part 1, via
57	  `injective_toPresheafOfModules`) with the free resolution `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
58	  (Lane 1) transported onto the section Čech complex through `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso`.
59	
60	- `ses_cech_h1` (**proved**, project-local Mathlib supplement below): the surjectivity on
61	  sections induced by a short exact sequence of sheaves with Čech-H¹-vanishing kernel
62	  (Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`), via the cocycle-to-coboundary heart
63	  `sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology` and sheaf gluing.
64	
65	- `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` / `affine_serre_vanishing` (planned): the comparison
66	  isomorphism `Ȟ•(𝒰, F) ≅ H•(X, F)` on affine schemes / for acyclic covers, leading to
67	  Serre's vanishing theorem.
68	-/
69	
70	universe u
71	
72	open CategoryTheory Limits
73	
74	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
75	
76	/-
77	Planner strategy for `cechComplex_hom_identification`:
78	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
79	
80	Goal: construct a cochain-complex isomorphism
81	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`
82	where:
83	  • `K(𝒰)_p = ∐_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
84	    (the degree-`p` term of `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`)
85	  • `Č^p(𝒰, F) = ∏_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} F.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
86	    (the degree-`p` term of `sectionCechComplex 𝒰 F`)
87	  Both complexes live in `Ab` after applying the global-sections functor.
88	
89	Step 1 — per-degree hom-coproduct duality:
90	  For each `p`, the Yoneda/free-presheaf adjunction gives
91	    `Hom(freeYoneda.obj V, F) ≅ F.obj V`  (natural in `V`).
92	  This is exactly `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` from `PresheafCech.lean`.
93	  Combined with the coproduct-hom identity
94	    `Hom(∐_σ A_σ, F) ≅ ∏_σ Hom(A_σ, F)`
95	  (via `preadditiveYoneda` preservation of limits, or hand-rolled from
96	  `Limits.Sigma.desc` / `Limits.Sigma.ι`), we get a degree-wise `Ab`-iso
97	    `Hom(K(𝒰)_p, F) ≅ ∏_σ F.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) = Č^p(𝒰, F)`.
98	
99	Step 2 — differential intertwining:
100	  Show that the degree-wise isos commute with the differentials.  The differential on
101	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)` is precomposition with the Čech boundary `d_p : K(𝒰)_{p+1} → K(𝒰)_p`
102	  (the alternating sum of face maps of `cechFreeSimplicial`).  The differential on
103	  `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the alternating sum of restriction maps built by `sectionCechComplex`.
104	  These match under the per-degree iso by naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
105	
106	Step 3 — assemble:
107	  Use `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents` (or equivalent) to combine the per-degree
108	  isos into a full cochain-complex iso, supplying the naturality squares from Step 2.
109	
110	Key API:
111	  • `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` (PresheafCech.lean): the per-degree iso.
112	  • `Limits.Sigma.desc`, `Limits.Sigma.ι`: coproduct universal property in
113	    `X.PresheafOfModules`.
114	  • `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`: assemble component-wise isos into a
115	    full complex iso.
116	  • `sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean): the target complex.
117	  • `cechFreePresheafComplex` / `cechFreePresheafComplex_X` (FreePresheafComplex.lean):
118	    the source complex and its degreewise unfolding lemma.
119	-/
120	
121	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech hom-identification -/
122	
123	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
124	
125	/-- **The hom cosimplicial abelian group `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)`.**
126	
127	The cosimplicial object in `Ab` obtained by applying the contravariant additive functor
128	`Hom_{PMod}(-, F) = preadditiveYoneda.obj F` to the free Čech simplicial object
129	`cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰`.  Its value in degree `p` is the hom-group
130	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_p, F)`, and its alternating-coface-map cochain complex is the left-hand
131	side of the Čech hom-identification.  Defining it this way makes the differential of the
132	hom-complex literally the alternating sum of `Hom(faceᵢ, F)`, so the identification with
133	`sectionCechComplex` reduces to a cosimplicial natural isomorphism.
134	
135	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `cechFreeSimplicial` nor this contravariant transport. -/
136	noncomputable def homCechCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
137	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} :=
138	  (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).rightOp ⋙ preadditiveYoneda.obj F
139	
140	/-- **The hom cochain complex `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)`** — the left-hand side of the Čech
141	hom-identification.
142	
143	The alternating-coface-map cochain complex of `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F`. Built the same way as
144	`sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean) so that the planned identification
145	`cechComplex_hom_identification : homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F` is just
146	`(alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).mapIso` of the cosimplicial natural isomorphism.
147	
148	Project-local: the hom-complex of the free Čech resolution has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
149	noncomputable def homCechComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
150	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
151	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F)
152	
153	/-- **Per-degree component of the Čech hom-identification.**
154	
155	For each simplicial degree `n`, the abelian-group isomorphism
156	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_n, F) ≅ ∏_σ F(⨅ₖ U(σ k))` assembled from (i) the coproduct–hom duality
157	`Hom(∐_σ Aσ, F) ≅ ∏_σ Hom(Aσ, F)` (the additive functor `Hom(-, F)` preserves the limit,
158	so `piComparison` is an iso, combined with `opCoproductIsoProduct`), and (ii) the per-index
159	free–Yoneda section identification `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
160	
161	Project-local: this is the degreewise core of `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`. -/
162	noncomputable def homCechSectionIsoApp (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
163	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (n : SimplexCategory) :
164	    (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F).obj n ≅ (sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F).obj n :=
165	  (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapIso
166	      (opCoproductIsoProduct
167	        (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))
168	    ≪≫ asIso (piComparison (preadditiveYoneda.obj F)
169	        (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => Opposite.op (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))))
170	    ≪≫ Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
171	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) F).toAddCommGrpIso)
172	
173	/-- `Pi.mapIso e` is by definition `Pi.map (fun b => (e b).hom)`. -/
174	private lemma pi_mapIso_hom_eq {β : Type*} {C : Type*} [Category C] {f g : β → C}
175	    [HasProductsOfShape β C] (e : ∀ b, f b ≅ g b) :
176	    (Limits.Pi.mapIso e).hom = Limits.Pi.map (fun b => (e b).hom) := rfl
177	
178	/-- Characterizing property of `homCechSectionIsoApp`: its `σ`-component is precomposition
179	with the coproduct injection `Sigma.ι σ` followed by the free–Yoneda section identification.
180	Reduces the naturality of the assembled iso to the naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`. -/
181	private lemma homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
182	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (n : SimplexCategory) (σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
183	    (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F n).hom ≫
184	        Pi.π (fun τ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
185	          F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))) σ =
186	      (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map
187	          (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
188	            freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) σ).op ≫
189	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom := by
190	  rw [homCechSectionIsoApp, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,
191	    pi_mapIso_hom_eq]
192	  simp only [Category.assoc]
193	  erw [Pi.map_π, piComparison_comp_π_assoc]
194	  rw [← Category.assoc]
195	  congr 1
196	  exact ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
197	    (congrArg _ (opCoproductIsoProduct_hom_comp_π σ))
198	
199	/-- **Naturality of the free–Yoneda section identification in the open.**
200	
201	For an inclusion of opens `h : V ⟶ W`, precomposition with `freeYoneda.map h` on the hom-side
202	corresponds to restriction `F.presheaf.map h.op` on the section side.  This is the single
203	naturality square that powers the cosimplicial naturality of the Čech hom-identification. -/
204	private lemma freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality {V W : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X}
205	    (h : V ⟶ W) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
206	    (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map (freeYoneda.map h).op ≫
207	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv V F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom
208	      = (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv W F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom ≫ F.presheaf.map h.op := by
209	  ext ψ
210	  show freeYonedaHomEquiv V F (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ)
211	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.presheaf.map h.op)) (freeYonedaHomEquiv W F ψ)
212	  show yonedaEquiv (PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ))
213	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.presheaf.map h.op))
214	          (yonedaEquiv (PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv ψ))
215	  rw [show PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ)
216	        = yoneda.map h ≫ PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv ψ from
217	      (PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction _).homEquiv_naturality_left (yoneda.map h) ψ,
218	    ← yonedaEquiv_naturality]
219	  rfl
220	
221	/-- **The cosimplicial natural isomorphism `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.**
222	
223	Assembles the per-degree isomorphisms `homCechSectionIsoApp` into a natural isomorphism of
224	cosimplicial abelian groups. The naturality square reduces, via `homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π`
225	in both degrees, to the single naturality square `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality` of the
226	free–Yoneda section identification.
227	
228	Project-local: the cosimplicial comparison of the hom-complex with the section complex has no
229	Mathlib counterpart. -/
230	noncomputable def homCechSectionCosimplicialIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
231	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
232	    homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
233	  NatIso.ofComponents (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F) (by
234	    intro n m f
235	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
236	    intro σ
237	    dsimp only [sectionCechCosimplicial]
238	    rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π]
239	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π 𝒰 F m σ]
240	    conv_rhs => rw [← Category.assoc]
241	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π 𝒰 F n (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f))]
242	    erw [Category.assoc]
243	    erw [← freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality
244	        (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f) σ)) F]
245	    dsimp only [homCechCosimplicial, Functor.comp_map, Functor.rightOp_map]
246	    erw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
247	    congr 1
248	    congr 1
249	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
250	    simp only [unop_comp]
251	    dsimp only [cechFreeSimplicial]
252	    erw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
253	    rfl
254	    )
255	
256	/-- **The Čech hom-identification** (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`).
257	
258	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)` identifying the
259	hom-complex of the free Čech resolution with the section Čech complex of `F`. Obtained by
260	applying the alternating-coface-map complex functor to the cosimplicial natural isomorphism
261	`homCechSectionCosimplicialIso`, so the differential intertwining is automatic from the
262	cosimplicial naturality.
263	
264	Project-local: the comparison of the free-resolution hom-complex with the section complex has
265	no Mathlib counterpart. -/
266	noncomputable def cechComplex_hom_identification (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
267	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
268	    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
269	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).mapIso (homCechSectionCosimplicialIso 𝒰 F)
270	
271	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — hom-complex as contravariant transport
272	
273	The injective-acyclicity assembly (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, gated on Lane-1's
274	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`) needs `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` — the alternating *coface* complex of
275	the hom-cosimplicial object `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F` — to be identified with the
276	contravariant transport `Hom(-, F) = preadditiveYoneda.obj F` of the *opposite* of the free
277	Čech chain complex `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`.  Once this identification is in hand,
278	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (applied to `(cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).op`) and
279	`cechComplex_hom_identification` combine in a single step to turn the free resolution into
280	Čech acyclicity of injective sheaves.
281	
282	Project-local: the comparison of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped opposite
283	of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
284	
285	/-- The `i`-th coface of `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F` is `Hom(-, F)` applied to the opposite of
286	the `i`-th face of `cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰`.  Holds definitionally (both unfold to
287	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).map (SimplexCategory.δ i).op).op`);
288	isolated as a `rfl` lemma to drive the differential identification `homCechComplex_d_eq`. -/
289	private lemma homCechCosimplicial_δ (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
290	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {n : ℕ} (i : Fin (n + 2)) :
291	    (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F).δ i
292	      = (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i).op :=
293	  rfl
294	
295	/-- **Degreewise differential identification** of the hom-complex with the mapped opposite of
296	the free Čech complex.  Both differentials are alternating sums of (co)faces; pushing the
297	opposite and the additive functor `Hom(-, F)` through the alternating sum on the right and
298	using `homCechCosimplicial_δ` matches them term by term.  This is the naturality input for the
299	cochain-complex isomorphism `homCechComplexMapOpIso`. -/
300	private lemma homCechComplex_d_eq (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
301	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
302	    (homCechComplex 𝒰 F).d p (p + 1)
303	      = (((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
304	          (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰))).d p (p + 1) := by
305	  have hL : (homCechComplex 𝒰 F).d p (p + 1)
306	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F) p :=
307	    CochainComplex.of_d (fun n => (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk n)) (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F)) p
308	  have hR : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d (p + 1) p
309	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) p :=
310	    ChainComplex.of_d (fun n => (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk n))) (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)) p
311	  rw [hL, AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD,
312	    Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, HomologicalComplex.op_d, hR,
313	    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD]
314	  have hop := CategoryTheory.op_sum
315	    ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk (p + 1))))
316	    ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
317	    (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (p + 2)))
318	    (fun i => (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i)
319	  erw [hop, Functor.map_sum]
320	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
321	  rw [homCechCosimplicial_δ, CategoryTheory.op_zsmul]
322	  erw [Functor.map_zsmul]
323	  rfl
324	
325	/-- **Hom-complex as contravariant transport of the free Čech complex.**
326	
327	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Hom(-, F)((K(𝒰)_•)ᵒᵖ)` identifying the
328	alternating-coface hom-complex `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` with the contravariant transport
329	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex` of the opposite of the free Čech chain
330	complex `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`.  Degreewise the two complexes have the *same* term
331	(`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_p, F)`), so the components are identities; the differential squares are
332	`homCechComplex_d_eq`.
333	
334	This is the bridge that turns Lane-1's free resolution into Čech acyclicity: applying
335	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` to `(cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).op` produces a
336	quasi-isomorphism of mapped-opposite complexes, and this iso transports it onto
337	`homCechComplex 𝒰 F` (hence, via `cechComplex_hom_identification`, onto `sectionCechComplex`).
338	
339	Project-local: the identification of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped
340	opposite of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
341	noncomputable def homCechComplexMapOpIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
342	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
343	    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅
344	      ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
345	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰)) :=
346	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by
347	    rintro i j (rfl : i + 1 = j)
348	    exact (Category.id_comp _).trans
349	      ((homCechComplex_d_eq 𝒰 F i).symm.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm))
350	
351	/-- **Section Čech complex as contravariant transport of the free Čech complex.**
352	
353	Composing `homCechComplexMapOpIso` with the Čech hom-identification
354	`cechComplex_hom_identification` gives a cochain-complex isomorphism between the mapped
355	opposite of the free Čech complex and the section Čech complex `Č•(coverOpen 𝒰, F)`.  This is
356	the single isomorphism needed for the injective-acyclicity assembly: once Lane 1 provides
357	`QuasiIso (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰)`, mapping its opposite through `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`
358	(quasi-iso by `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective`) and transporting the source/target
359	across this iso identifies `sectionCechComplex` with the mapped opposite of a complex
360	concentrated in degree `0`, giving positive-degree vanishing.
361	
362	Project-local: assembled from two project-local isomorphisms. -/
363	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMapOpIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
364	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
365	    ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
366	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰))
367	      ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
368	  (homCechComplexMapOpIso 𝒰 F).symm ≪≫ cechComplex_hom_identification 𝒰 F
369	
370	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Hom(-, I)` is exact for injective `I`
371	
372	The presheaf-level Čech-acyclicity argument (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`) needs the single
373	categorical fact that, for an injective object `I` of an abelian category, the contravariant
374	hom functor `Hom(-, I)` is exact — equivalently, the covariant functor
375	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab` preserves homology. Mathlib supplies the two pieces:
376	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I` always preserves finite limits (it is left exact), and injectivity
377	is equivalent to it preserving epimorphisms; we assemble these into preservation of finite
378	colimits (hence of homology), and feed the result through
379	`HomologicalComplex.quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` to obtain quasi-isomorphism
380	preservation. These are stated for a general abelian category so they apply verbatim to
381	`X.PresheafOfModules` once the injectivity of `I` as a presheaf of modules is in hand.
382	
383	Project-local: Mathlib has the ingredients but not the packaged exactness of `Hom(-, I)` as a
384	homology-preserving functor. -/
385	
386	section InjectiveHomExact
387	
388	open CategoryTheory Limits
389	
390	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
391	
392	/-- **`Hom(-, I)` preserves finite colimits for injective `I`.** For an injective object `I`
393	of an abelian category, the covariant functor `preadditiveYoneda.obj I : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab`
394	preserves finite colimits: it is left exact (preserves finite limits) automatically, and
395	injectivity makes it preserve epimorphisms, which together with left exactness gives
396	exactness. Combined with the automatic `PreservesFiniteLimits` instance this yields
397	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesHomology` by `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact`.
398	
399	Project-local: the exactness of `Hom(-, I)` for injective `I` is the categorical engine of
400	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`. -/
401	instance preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective
402	    (I : C) [Injective I] : PreservesFiniteColimits (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) := by
403	  have hepi : (preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesEpimorphisms :=
404	    (Injective.injective_iff_preservesEpimorphisms_preadditiveYoneda_obj I).mp inferInstance
405	  rw [Functor.preservesFiniteColimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_epi]
406	  intro S hS
407	  have hg := hS.epi_g
408	  exact ⟨hS.exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel _ hS.mono_f inferInstance,
409	    hepi.preserves S.g⟩
410	
411	/-- **`Hom(-, I)` carries quasi-isomorphisms to quasi-isomorphisms for injective `I`.**
412	Since `preadditiveYoneda.obj I` preserves homology
413	(`preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective`), applying it degreewise to a
414	quasi-isomorphism `φ` of chain complexes in `Cᵒᵖ` produces a quasi-isomorphism of the
415	`Hom`-cochain complexes. This is the form consumed by `injective_cech_acyclic`: the
416	augmentation `cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰` (a quasi-isomorphism once Lane 1 lands), taken opposite
417	and mapped through `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`, stays a quasi-isomorphism.
418	
419	Project-local: packages `quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` against the injective-hom
420	exactness instance above. -/
421	lemma quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective (I : C) [Injective I]
422	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {K L : HomologicalComplex Cᵒᵖ c}
423	    (φ : K ⟶ L) [QuasiIso φ] :
424	    QuasiIso (((preadditiveYoneda.obj I).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) := by
425	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
426	  intro i
427	  exact HomologicalComplex.quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology φ (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) i
428	    (hφ := QuasiIso.quasiIsoAt (f := φ) i)
429	
430	end InjectiveHomExact
431	
432	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech `H¹` vanishing ⟹ cocycles are coboundaries
433	
434	The surjectivity-on-sections step `ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks
435	`lemma-ses-cech-h1`) consumes the {\v C}ech-algebra fact that a covering with
436	`Ȟ¹(𝒰, F) = 0` has *every* {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle equal to a coboundary. This is the
437	`\uses{def:cech_complex}` content of that lemma, isolated here as a self-contained
438	statement about the section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (no sheaf gluing or
439	local-surjectivity input). It is the converse direction of
440	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` (CechAcyclic), packaged in section
441	coordinates via `sectionCechProductEquiv` / `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
442	
443	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
444	neither the homology-to-exactness reduction nor its coordinate form exist there. -/
445	
446	section CechH1Coboundary
447	
448	open AlgebraicTopology
449	
450	/-- **Homology vanishing ⟹ coface-differential exactness** (converse of
451	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`). If the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the
452	section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, the underlying group homomorphisms of the two
453	consecutive coface differentials `objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence. Pure
454	homological algebra: the same `exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` / `exactAt_iff'` /
455	`ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact` chain, run in the extraction direction.
456	
457	Project-local: extraction form of the `Ab`-side homology bridge of
458	`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`, needed by the `ses_cech_h1` coboundary step. -/
459	theorem sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
460	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
461	    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1))) :
462	    Function.Exact
463	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
464	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
465	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1))) := by
466	  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
467	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
468	    CochainComplex.of_d (fun n => (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk n)) (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)) q
469	  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
470	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
471	    CochainComplex.of_d (fun n => (sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk n)) (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)) (q + 1)
472	  have key : Function.Exact
473	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
474	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2))) := by
475	    have hh := h
476	    rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
477	        (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
478	        ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hh
479	    exact hh
480	  rw [hf, hg] at key
481	  exact key
482	
483	/-- **Čech `1`-cocycles are coboundaries when `Ȟ¹(𝒰, F) = 0`** — the
484	`\uses{def:cech_complex}` heart of `ses_cech_h1`.
485	
486	Working in the section coordinates of `sectionCechCosimplicial` (a family of sections
487	`c σ ∈ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` indexed by double-indices `σ : Fin 2 → ι`), if the family
488	satisfies the {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle identity (the alternating sum of its restrictions
489	over each triple-index `σ : Fin 3 → ι` vanishes) and the degree-`1` homology of the
490	section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, then `c` is a coboundary: there is a `0`-cochain
491	`t` (sections `t σ ∈ F(U (σ 0))` over single-indices) with `c σ` the alternating sum
492	of the face restrictions of `t`, i.e. `c σ = t_{σ 1}|_{σ} - t_{σ 0}|_{σ}` once the
493	two-term sum is expanded. The restriction maps are `sectionCechFaceRestr`, exactly the
494	{\v C}ech coface restrictions appearing in `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
495	
496	Project-local: the section-coordinate coboundary extraction for the section {\v C}ech
497	complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
498	theorem sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
499	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
500	    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
501	    (c : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
502	    (hcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι,
503	      ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
504	        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
505	          (c (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0) :
506	    ∃ t : ∀ σ : Fin 1 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))),
507	      ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, c σ = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
508	        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
509	          (t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
510	  have he := sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology U F 0 h
511	  set c' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 1)) :=
512	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).symm c with hc'
513	  have hpe1 : sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1 c' = c :=
514	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).apply_symm_apply c
515	  have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
516	      (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) 1) c' = 0 := by
517	    apply (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2).injective
518	    funext σ
519	    rw [sectionCech_objD_apply U F 1 c' σ, hpe1]
520	    rw [show sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2 0 σ = 0 from by
521	      rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; exact map_zero _]
522	    exact hcoc σ
523	  obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := (he c').mp hzero
524	  refine ⟨sectionCechProductEquiv U F 0 b, fun σ => ?_⟩
525	  rw [← hpe1, ← hb, sectionCech_objD_apply U F 0 b σ]
526	
527	end CechH1Coboundary
528	
529	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¹`
530	vanishing (`ses_cech_h1`)
531	
532	This section assembles the {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing into the surjectivity statement
533	`ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).  The
534	{\v C}ech-algebra heart is `sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology` (the
535	cocycle-to-coboundary extraction proven above); the remaining content is the standard
536	sheaf theory — local lifting, left-exactness on sections, and gluing — which here is
537	supplied through Mathlib's `TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.isSheafUniqueGluing` and a handful of
538	restriction-bookkeeping helpers.
539	
540	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
541	the surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is new here. -/
542	
543	section SesCechH1
544	
545	open AlgebraicTopology TopologicalSpace Opposite
546	
547	/-- **Composite of two presheaf restrictions is the direct restriction.**
548	For `A ≤ B ≤ C` the restriction `P(C) → P(B)` followed by `P(B) → P(A)` equals the direct
549	restriction `P(C) → P(A)`.  Holds definitionally for `Ab`-valued presheaves on opens
550	because the composite of the opposite `homOfLE`s is the opposite `homOfLE` of the
551	composite.  Used pervasively to collapse restriction chains in `ses_cech_h1`. -/
552	private lemma restr_trans (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {A B C : Opens ↥X}
553	    (h1 : A ≤ B) (h2 : B ≤ C) (x : ToType (P.obj (op C))) :
554	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h1).op)
555	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h2).op) x)
556	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE (h1.trans h2)).op) x := by
557	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← P.map_comp, ← op_comp]
558	  rfl
559	
560	/-- **Restriction between two equal opens is injective.**  When `V ≤ W` and `W ≤ V`
561	(i.e. `V = W`) the restriction `P(W) → P(V)` is injective: the reverse restriction is a
562	two-sided inverse (`restr_trans` collapses the round trip to the identity, by uniqueness of
563	poset morphisms).  Used to transport the {\v C}ech compatibility equation between the binary
564	intersection `U i ⊓ U j` and the {\v C}ech double-overlap `⨅ₖ U (![i,j] k)`. -/
565	private lemma restr_inj_of_eq (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {V W : Opens ↥X}
566	    (h : V ≤ W) (h' : W ≤ V) :
567	    Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h).op)) := by
568	  intro a b hab
569	  have h2 := congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h').op)) hab
570	  rw [restr_trans, restr_trans] at h2
571	  rwa [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE (h'.trans h)) (𝟙 _), op_id, P.map_id,
572	    ConcreteCategory.id_apply, ConcreteCategory.id_apply] at h2
573	
574	/-- **Two parallel restrictions agree.**  Any two restriction maps `P(A) → P(C)` (opposite
575	opens morphisms) act identically, by uniqueness of poset morphisms.  Used to discharge the
576	final defeq-up-to-proof-irrelevance step after collapsing restriction chains with
577	`← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp`. -/
578	private lemma restr_op_unique (P : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u}) {A C : Opens ↥X}
579	    (f g : Opposite.op C ⟶ Opposite.op A) (x : ToType (P.obj (Opposite.op C))) :
580	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map f) x = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map g) x := by
581	  rw [show f = g from Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (Subsingleton.elim _ _)]
582	
583	/-- **Restriction of a single-index {\v C}ech section is invariant under reindexing the
584	tuple.**  For two equal `Fin 1`-tuples `ρ = ρ'` (e.g. `fun _ => i` and `![i,j] ∘ δ`), the
585	restrictions of `g' ρ` and `g' ρ'` to a common open `W` agree.  Proved by `subst` (so the
586	propositional-but-not-definitional tuple equality is discharged without a heterogeneous
587	rewrite), then `restr_op_unique`.  This is the bridge between the `U`-indexed glue family
588	(`fun _ => i`) and the {\v C}ech double-overlap agreement (`![i,j] ∘ δ`). -/
589	private lemma restr_g'_transport {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (G : X.PresheafOfModules)
590	    (g' : ∀ ρ : Fin 1 → ι, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))))
591	    {W : Opens ↥X} {ρ ρ' : Fin 1 → ι} (hρ : ρ = ρ')
592	    (f : Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)) ⟶ Opposite.op W)
593	    (f' : Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (ρ' k)) ⟶ Opposite.op W) :
594	    ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map f) (g' ρ)
595	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map f') (g' ρ') := by
596	  subst hρ
597	  exact restr_op_unique G.presheaf f f' (g' ρ)
598	
599	/-- **A morphism of presheaves of modules commutes with the {\v C}ech face restrictions.**
600	For `fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf` the section-level map `fι.app` intertwines the
601	{\v C}ech face restriction `sectionCechFaceRestr` of `F` with that of `G`; this is just
602	the naturality of `fι`.  It is the single naturality input that pushes the cocycle and
603	coboundary identities of the heart between `F` and `G`. -/
604	private lemma fι_sectionCechFaceRestr {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
605	    (F G : X.PresheafOfModules) (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf)
606	    {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2))
607	    (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj
608	      (op (⨅ l, U ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l))))) :
609	    ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
610	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _) x)
611	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app _)
612	          (ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i) x) := by
613	  unfold sectionCechFaceRestr
614	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, fι.naturality]
615	
616	/-- The single-index {\v C}ech intersection at a constant tuple is the cover member. -/
617	private lemma coverConst_iInf {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (i : ι) :
618	    (⨅ k : Fin 1, U ((fun _ => i) k)) = U i := by simp
619	
620	/-- The double-index {\v C}ech intersection at `![i, j]` is the binary intersection. -/
621	private lemma coverPair_iInf {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X) (i j : ι) :
622	    (⨅ k : Fin 2, U (![i, j] k)) = U i ⊓ U j := by
623	  apply le_antisymm
624	  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (iInf_le _ 1)
625	  · exact le_iInf (fun k => by fin_cases k <;> simp)
626	
627	/-- The `0`-th face of `![i, j]` is the constant tuple `j`. -/
628	private lemma pair_comp_δ0 {ι : Type u} (i j : ι) :
629	    (![i, j] ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (0 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => j) := by
630	  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.δ, Fin.succAbove]
631	
632	/-- The `1`-st face of `![i, j]` is the constant tuple `i`. -/
633	private lemma pair_comp_δ1 {ι : Type u} (i j : ι) :
634	    (![i, j] ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ (1 : Fin 2)).toOrderHom) = (fun _ => i) := by
635	  funext k; fin_cases k; simp [SimplexCategory.δ, Fin.succAbove]
636	
637	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
638	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
639	/-- **Surjectivity on sections from {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing** (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`,
640	Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`).
641	
642	For a short exact sequence of presheaves of modules `0 → F → G → H → 0` on a scheme `X`
643	— presented through the underlying `Ab`-presheaf maps `fι`, `gπ`, with `fι` a monomorphism
644	on sections (`hmono`), `gπ ∘ fι = 0` (`hπι`), and every kernel section of `gπ` in the image
645	of `fι` (`hker`) — together with an open cover `U` of the open `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` such that
646	`Ȟ¹(U, F) = 0` (`hH1`) and a family of local lifts `sLoc i ∈ G(Uᵢ)` of `s ∈ H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`
647	(`hlift`), the section `s` lies in the image of `G(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ) → H(⨆ᵢ Uᵢ)`.
648	
649	The differences `sⱼ|_{ij} − sᵢ|_{ij}` of the local lifts map to `0` in `H`, hence lift to a
650	{\v C}ech `1`-cocycle in `F` (`hker`); the {\v C}ech-`H¹` vanishing makes it a coboundary
651	(`sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology`); subtracting the coboundary makes the
652	corrected lifts agree on overlaps, and the sheaf condition on `G` glues them to a global
653	section of `G` over `⨆ᵢ Uᵢ` mapping to `s` (separatedness of `H` identifies its image with
654	`s`).  The "cofinal system of covers" of the Stacks statement is captured here by taking a
655	single cover satisfying both `Ȟ¹ = 0` and the local-lift property as hypotheses.
656	
657	Project-local: the {\v C}ech surjectivity criterion phrased through `sectionCechComplex` is
658	new here (Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules). -/
659	theorem ses_cech_h1 {ι : Type u} (U : ι → Opens ↥X)
660	    (F G H : X.PresheafOfModules)
661	    (fι : F.presheaf ⟶ G.presheaf) (gπ : G.presheaf ⟶ H.presheaf)
662	    (hGsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) G.presheaf)
663	    (hHsh : TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf (X := X.toTopCat) H.presheaf)
664	    (hπι : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (F.presheaf.obj V)),
665	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0)
666	    (hmono : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ), Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)))
667	    (hker : ∀ (V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (G.presheaf.obj V)),
668	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x)
669	    (s : ToType (H.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))))
670	    (hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
671	    (sLoc : ∀ i, ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (U i))))
672	    (hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (U i))) (sLoc i)
673	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (Opens.leSupr U i).op) s) :
674	    ∃ g : ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (iSup U))),
675	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (iSup U))) g = s := by
676	  classical
677	  -- single-index local sections, recast in ⨅-coordinates
678	  set sLoc'coord : (σ : Fin 1 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
679	    fun σ => ConcreteCategory.hom (G.presheaf.map (homOfLE (iInf_le _ 0)).op) (sLoc (σ 0))
680	    with hsLoc'coord
681	  set sLoc' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U G).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) :=
682	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).symm sLoc'coord with hsLoc'
683	  have hpe0 : sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0 sLoc' = sLoc'coord :=
684	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U G 0).apply_symm_apply sLoc'coord
685	  -- the degree-1 cochain of G-differences
686	  set cyc1 := ConcreteCategory.hom
687	    (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U G) 0) sLoc' with hcyc1
688	  set dGcoord : (σ : Fin 2 → ι) → ToType (G.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) :=
689	    fun σ => sectionCechProductEquiv U G 1 cyc1 σ with hdGcoord
690	  have hdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, dGcoord σ
691	      = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
692	          ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
693	            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
694	    intro σ
695	    have happ := sectionCech_objD_apply U G 0 sLoc' σ
696	    rw [hpe0] at happ
697	    exact happ
698	  -- `gπ` carries a single ⨅-coordinate local section to the restriction of `s`
699	  have hgπsLoc' : ∀ (ρ : Fin 1 → ι) (h : (⨅ k, U (ρ k)) ≤ iSup U),
700	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (ρ k)))) (sLoc'coord ρ)
701	        = ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op) s := by
702	    intro ρ h
703	    rw [hsLoc'coord, ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, gπ.naturality,
704	      ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, hlift (ρ 0)]
705	    exact restr_trans H.presheaf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U (ρ 0)) s
706	  -- `gπ` kills the differences
707	  have hgπdG : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι,
708	      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) (dGcoord σ) = 0 := by
709	    intro σ
710	    rw [hdG, map_sum]
711	    have key : ∀ i : Fin 2,
712	        ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
713	          ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U G σ i)
714	            (sLoc'coord (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)))
715	        = (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • ConcreteCategory.hom (H.presheaf.map
716	            (homOfLE (le_trans (iInf_le (fun k => U (σ k)) (0 : Fin 2)) (le_iSup U (σ 0)))).op) s := by
717	      intro i
718	      rw [map_zsmul]; congr 1
719	      rw [← fι_sectionCechFaceRestr U G H gπ σ i,
720	        hgπsLoc' (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) (le_trans (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iSup U _))]

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FreePresheafComplex
9	
10	/-!
11	# Form-B absolute cohomology `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p(jShriekOU U, F)` (P5b input)
12	-/
13	
14	universe u
15	
16	open CategoryTheory Limits CategoryTheory.Abelian
17	
18	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
19	
20	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
21	
22	/-- The standard `HasExt` structure on `X.Modules`, made a section-local instance so the
23	`Ext`-based absolute cohomology below resolves it without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom`
24	typeclass search. -/
25	noncomputable local instance hasExtModules : HasExt.{u + 1, u, u + 1} X.Modules :=
26	  HasExt.standard _
27	
28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Form-B absolute cohomology -/
29	
30	noncomputable def jShriekOU (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.Modules :=
31	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).obj
32	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U))
33	
34	/-- The sheafification adjunction hom-bijection as an `AddEquiv` (additivity holds because
35	the right adjoint is an additive functor and composition is bilinear). -/
36	noncomputable def sheafificationHomAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
37	    (jShriekOU U ⟶ F) ≃+
38	      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U) ⟶
39	        (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) :=
40	  { (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv
41	      ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj U)) F with
42	    map_add' := by
43	      intro f g
44	      haveI : (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
45	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).Additive := inferInstance
46	      simp only [Equiv.toFun_as_coe, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]
47	      erw [Functor.map_add, Preadditive.comp_add]
48	      rfl }
49	
50	noncomputable def jShriekOU_homEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.Modules) :
51	    (jShriekOU U ⟶ F) ≃+
52	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
53	  (sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F).trans (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv U _)
54	
55	/-- **Form-B absolute cohomology** `H^p(U, F) := Ext^p_{O_X}(jShriekOU U, F)`. -/
56	noncomputable def absoluteCohomology (p : ℕ) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
57	    (F : X.Modules) : AddCommGrpCat :=
58	  AddCommGrpCat.of (Ext (jShriekOU U) F p)
59	
60	/-- **`H⁰(U, F) ≅ Γ(U, F)`** as abelian groups: degree-zero `Ext` out of the
61	corepresenting object `jShriekOU U` recovers the sections of `F` over `U`. This is the
62	additive corepresentability of global sections specialised to `p = 0`. -/
63	noncomputable def absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
64	    (F : X.Modules) :
65	    Ext (jShriekOU U) F 0 ≃+
66	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U) :=
67	  (AddEquiv.mk' Ext.homEquiv₀ (by
68	    intro a b
69	    refine Ext.homEquiv₀.symm.injective ?_
70	    simp only [Ext.homEquiv₀_symm_apply, Ext.mk₀_add, Ext.mk₀_homEquiv₀_apply])).trans
71	    (jShriekOU_homEquiv U F)
72	
73	/-- **Injective vanishing**: positive-degree absolute cohomology of an injective sheaf of
74	modules vanishes. Direct from `Ext.eq_zero_of_injective` (the injective object is the
75	*second* `Ext` argument, so no restriction of the coefficient sheaf is needed). -/
76	theorem absoluteCohomology_eq_zero_of_injective (n : ℕ) (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
77	    (I : X.Modules) [Injective I] (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) I (n + 1)) : e = 0 :=
78	  Ext.eq_zero_of_injective e
79	
80	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, surjectivity at `H^{n₁}(U, F₁)`.**
81	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁` at fixed first argument
82	`jShriekOU U`, for a short exact sequence of `O_X`-modules. -/
83	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₁ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
84	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n₁ : ℕ}
85	    (x₁ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₁ n₁) (hx₁ : x₁.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n₁) = 0)
86	    {n₀ : ℕ} (hn₀ : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
87	    ∃ x₃ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₃ n₀, x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₀ = x₁ :=
88	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ (jShriekOU U) hS x₁ hx₁ hn₀
89	
90	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, exactness at `H^n(U, F₂)`.**
91	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂` at fixed first argument
92	`jShriekOU U`. -/
93	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₂ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
94	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n : ℕ}
95	    (x₂ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₂ n) (hx₂ : x₂.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n) = 0) :
96	    ∃ x₁ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₁ n, x₁.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n) = x₂ :=
97	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂ (jShriekOU U) hS x₂ hx₂
98	
99	/-- **Covariant `H^p(U,-)` long exact sequence, exactness at the connecting map.**
100	Thin wrapper around `Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃` at fixed first argument
101	`jShriekOU U`. -/
102	theorem absoluteCohomology_covariant_exact₃ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
103	    {S : ShortComplex X.Modules} (hS : S.ShortExact) {n₀ : ℕ}
104	    (x₃ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₃ n₀) {n₁ : ℕ} (hn₁ : n₀ + 1 = n₁)
105	    (hx₃ : x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₁ = 0) :
106	    ∃ x₂ : Ext (jShriekOU U) S.X₂ n₀, x₂.comp (Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n₀) = x₃ :=
107	  Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ (jShriekOU U) hS x₃ hn₁ hx₃
108	
109	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — naturality of `H⁰ ≅ Γ` -/
110	
111	/-- **Naturality of `Ext.homEquiv₀` in the second argument.** The degree-zero `Ext`
112	comparison intertwines composition with the degree-zero Ext class `Ext.mk₀ g` and ordinary
113	post-composition with `g`. Project-local: Mathlib has the comparison `Ext.homEquiv₀` and the
114	composite formula `Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀`, but not this intertwining as a packaged lemma. -/
115	private lemma homEquiv₀_comp_mk₀ {A B Z : X.Modules} (e : Ext A B 0) (g : B ⟶ Z) :
116	    Ext.homEquiv₀ (e.comp (Ext.mk₀ g) (add_zero 0)) = Ext.homEquiv₀ e ≫ g := by
117	  conv_lhs => rw [← Ext.mk₀_homEquiv₀_apply e, Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀]
118	  rw [← Ext.homEquiv₀_symm_apply, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
119	
120	/-- **Naturality of `freeYonedaHomEquiv` in the coefficient presheaf.** Post-composition by a
121	morphism of presheaves of modules `q` corresponds, under the free–Yoneda evaluation bijection,
122	to applying `q` on sections over `V`. Project-local: the underlying bijection is project-local
123	(`freeYonedaHomEquiv`), so is its naturality. -/
124	private lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_naturality (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
125	    {F₁ F₂ : X.PresheafOfModules}
126	    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F₁) (q : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
127	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F₂ (ψ ≫ q)
128	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (q.app (Opposite.op V))) (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F₁ ψ) := by
129	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app,
130	    ModuleCat.comp_apply]
131	
132	/-- **Naturality of `sheafificationHomAddEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf.** This is the
133	naturality of the sheafification adjunction's hom-bijection in its right (codomain) argument;
134	the right adjoint is `Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X` (definitionally the forget–restrict
135	composite). Project-local because `sheafificationHomAddEquiv` is the project's additive
136	packaging of that bijection. -/
137	private lemma sheafificationHomAddEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
138	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU U ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
139	    sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F₂ (h ≫ g)
140	      = sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F₁ h ≫ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g :=
141	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).homEquiv_naturality_right h g
142	
143	/-- **Naturality of `jShriekOU_homEquiv` in the coefficient sheaf.** Composing a map
144	`jShriekOU U ⟶ F₁` with `g : F₁ ⟶ F₂` corresponds, under corepresentability, to applying `g` on
145	sections over `U`. Project-local: assembled from the naturality of the two project-local halves
146	(`sheafificationHomAddEquiv` and `freeYonedaHomEquiv`). -/
147	private lemma jShriekOU_homEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
148	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (h : jShriekOU U ⟶ F₁) (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) :
149	    jShriekOU_homEquiv U F₂ (h ≫ g)
150	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (Opposite.op U)))
151	          (jShriekOU_homEquiv U F₁ h) := by
152	  change freeYonedaHomEquiv U _ (sheafificationHomAddEquiv U F₂ (h ≫ g)) = _
153	  rw [sheafificationHomAddEquiv_naturality, freeYonedaHomEquiv_naturality]
154	  rfl
155	
156	/-- **Naturality of `H⁰(U,-) ≅ Γ(U,-)` in the coefficient sheaf**
157	(blueprint `lem:absolute_cohomology_zero_natural`). For `g : F₁ ⟶ F₂` in `X.Modules`, the
158	functorial action of `g` on `Ext⁰(jShriekOU U, -)` (composition with the degree-zero Ext class
159	`Ext.mk₀ g`) corresponds under the `H⁰ ≅ Γ` isomorphism to the sections map `g_U` over `U`.
160	In particular, when `g_U` is surjective so is `H⁰(U, g)` — the transfer used by the
161	surjectivity step of `lem:absolute_cohomology_one_vanishing`. -/
162	theorem absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv_naturality (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
163	    {F₁ F₂ : X.Modules} (g : F₁ ⟶ F₂) (e : Ext (jShriekOU U) F₁ 0) :
164	    absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U F₂ (e.comp (Ext.mk₀ g) (add_zero 0))
165	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).map g).app (Opposite.op U)))
166	          (absoluteCohomologyZeroAddEquiv U F₁ e) := by
167	  change jShriekOU_homEquiv U F₂ (Ext.homEquiv₀ (e.comp (Ext.mk₀ g) (add_zero 0))) = _
168	  rw [homEquiv₀_comp_mk₀, jShriekOU_homEquiv_naturality]
169	  rfl
170	
171	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechBridge
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.AbsoluteCohomology
8	
9	/-!
10	# Čech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis (Stacks 01EO) — L1/L2 chain
11	
12	Project-local: builds the section-Čech short-exact-sequence (L1) and the
13	quotient-vanishing step (L2) of the 01EO dimension-shift argument.
14	-/
15	
16	universe u
17	
18	open CategoryTheory Limits CategoryTheory.Abelian
19	
20	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
21	
22	-- Re-activate the (file-local) `HasExt` instance from `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` so that the
23	-- `Ext`-based absolute cohomology resolves here without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom` search.
24	attribute [local instance] hasExtModules
25	
26	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
27	
28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — functoriality of the section Čech complex -/
29	
30	/-- The cosimplicial morphism of section Čech objects induced by a morphism `φ` of
31	presheaves of modules, acting coordinatewise by the underlying presheaf morphism on each
32	basic-open section group. Project-local: `sectionCechCosimplicial` has no functoriality in
33	Mathlib. -/
34	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialMap {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
35	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (φ : F ⟶ G) :
36	    sectionCechCosimplicial U F ⟶ sectionCechCosimplicial U G where
37	  app n := Limits.Pi.map (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
38	    ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map φ).app (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
39	  naturality {m n} f := by
40	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
41	    intro σ
42	    simp only [sectionCechCosimplicial, Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.map_π, Limits.Pi.lift_π,
43	      Limits.Pi.map_π_assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π_assoc]
44	    congr 1
45	    exact ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map φ).naturality _
46	
47	/-- The section Čech cosimplicial object as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
48	Project-local: packages `sectionCechCosimplicialMap` with functoriality so the induced
49	short-complex maps compose and respect zero. -/
50	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor {ι : Type u}
51	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
52	  obj F := sectionCechCosimplicial U F
53	  map φ := sectionCechCosimplicialMap U φ
54	  map_id F := by
55	    apply NatTrans.ext
56	    funext n
57	    show (Limits.Pi.map fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
58	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (𝟙 F)).app
59	        (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) = 𝟙 _
60	    rw [show (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
61	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (𝟙 F)).app
62	          (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
63	      = (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
64	        𝟙 ((PresheafOfModules.presheaf F).obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) from by
65	        funext σ; rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]; rfl]
66	    exact Limits.Pi.map_id
67	  map_comp φ ψ := by
68	    apply NatTrans.ext
69	    funext n
70	    show (Limits.Pi.map fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
71	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (φ ≫ ψ)).app
72	        (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) = _
73	    rw [show (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
74	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map (φ ≫ ψ)).app
75	          (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
76	      = (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
77	        ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map φ).app (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))) ≫
78	          ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map ψ).app
79	            (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) from by funext σ; rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]; rfl]
80	    exact (Limits.Pi.map_comp_map _ _).symm
81	
82	/-- The section Čech cochain complex as a functor in the coefficient presheaf of modules.
83	Project-local: functoriality of `sectionCechComplex`, used to form the short exact sequence of
84	Čech complexes. -/
85	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexFunctor {ι : Type u}
86	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
87	  sectionCechCosimplicialFunctor U ⋙ AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}
88	
89	/-- The chain map of section Čech complexes induced by a morphism `φ` of presheaves of
90	modules. Project-local: functoriality of `sectionCechComplex` in the coefficient presheaf. -/
91	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMap {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
92	    {F G : X.PresheafOfModules} (φ : F ⟶ G) :
93	    sectionCechComplex U F ⟶ sectionCechComplex U G :=
94	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).map (sectionCechCosimplicialMap U φ)
95	
96	/-- **{\v C}ech cohomology accessor** `Ȟ^p(𝒰, F)`: the degree-`p` homology of the section
97	Čech complex, packaged as a named `Ab`-object. Project-local thin wrapper so the 01EO chain
98	can refer to `Ȟ^p` uniformly. -/
99	noncomputable def cechCohomology {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
100	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) : Ab.{u} :=
101	  (sectionCechComplex U F).homology p
102	
103	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — product of short exact sequences in `Ab` -/
104	
105	private lemma pi_π_map_apply {J : Type u} {f g : J → Ab.{u}} (φ : ∀ j, f j ⟶ g j)
106	    (x : ToType (∏ᶜ f)) (σ : J) :
107	    Limits.Pi.π g σ (Limits.Pi.map φ x) = φ σ (Limits.Pi.π f σ x) := by
108	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, Limits.Pi.map_π, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
109	
110	/-- **A product of short exact sequences of abelian groups is short exact.** This is the AB4*
111	content used in the section Čech short-exact-sequence: each Čech term is a product of section
112	groups over basic opens, so degreewise short-exactness of the Čech complexes reduces to this.
113	Project-local: Mathlib has no off-the-shelf "product of short exact sequences" lemma. -/
114	theorem shortExact_piMap {J : Type u} (S : J → ShortComplex Ab.{u}) (h : ∀ j, (S j).ShortExact) :
115	    (ShortComplex.mk (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).f)) (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).g))
116	      (by rw [Limits.Pi.map_comp_map]
117	          have hz : (fun j => (S j).f ≫ (S j).g) = (fun j => (0 : (S j).X₁ ⟶ (S j).X₃)) := by
118	            funext j; exact (S j).zero
119	          rw [hz]; ext x; simp)).ShortExact := by
120	  haveI : ∀ j, Mono (S j).f := fun j => (h j).mono_f
121	  -- Epi of the product map: surjectivity, chosen componentwise
122	  have hepi : Epi (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).g)) := by
123	    rw [AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective]
124	    intro y
125	    refine ⟨(Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X₂)).symm
126	      (fun σ => ((h σ).ab_surjective_g (Limits.Pi.π (fun j => (S j).X₃) σ y)).choose), ?_⟩
127	    refine (Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X₃)).injective (funext fun σ => ?_)
128	    rw [Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply, Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply,
129	      pi_π_map_apply, Concrete.productEquiv_symm_apply_π]
130	    exact ((h σ).ab_surjective_g (Limits.Pi.π (fun j => (S j).X₃) σ y)).choose_spec
131	  haveI := hepi
132	  haveI hmono : Mono (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).f)) := inferInstance
133	  refine ShortComplex.ShortExact.mk ?_
134	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact]
135	  show Function.Exact (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).f)) (Limits.Pi.map (fun j => (S j).g))
136	  intro x
137	  constructor
138	  · intro hx
139	    have hcomp : ∀ σ, (S σ).g (Limits.Pi.π (fun j => (S j).X₂) σ x) = 0 := by
140	      intro σ
141	      have := congrArg (Limits.Pi.π (fun j => (S j).X₃) σ) hx
142	      rwa [pi_π_map_apply, map_zero] at this
143	    choose w hw using fun σ => (((ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact (S σ)).mp
144	      (h σ).exact (Limits.Pi.π (fun j => (S j).X₂) σ x)).mp
145	      (hcomp σ) : Limits.Pi.π (fun j => (S j).X₂) σ x ∈ Set.range (S σ).f)
146	    refine ⟨(Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X₁)).symm w, ?_⟩
147	    refine (Concrete.productEquiv (fun j => (S j).X₂)).injective (funext fun σ => ?_)
148	    rw [Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply, Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply,
149	      pi_π_map_apply, Concrete.productEquiv_symm_apply_π]
150	    exact hw σ
151	  · rintro ⟨w, rfl⟩
152	    rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, Limits.Pi.map_comp_map]
153	    have hz : (fun j => (S j).f ≫ (S j).g) = (fun j => (0 : (S j).X₁ ⟶ (S j).X₃)) := by
154	      funext j; exact (S j).zero
155	    rw [hz, show (Limits.Pi.map fun j => (0 : (S j).X₁ ⟶ (S j).X₃)) = 0 from by ext z; simp]
156	    rfl
157	
158	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — L1 short exact sequence of section Čech complexes -/
159	
160	/-- The degree-`p`, index-`σ` term short complex of section groups over the basic open
161	`⨅ₖ U(σ k)` induced by a short complex `P` of presheaves of modules. Project-local: the
162	building block of the section Čech short exact sequence. -/
163	noncomputable def faceShortComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
164	    (P : ShortComplex X.PresheafOfModules) {p : ℕ} (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) : ShortComplex Ab.{u} :=
165	  ShortComplex.mk
166	    (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map P.f).app (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
167	    (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map P.g).app (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
168	    (by rw [← NatTrans.comp_app, ← CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp, P.zero,
169	          CategoryTheory.Functor.map_zero]; rfl)
170	
171	/-- The short complex of section Čech cochain complexes `0 → Č(F) → Č(I) → Č(Q) → 0` induced by
172	a short complex `P : 0 → F → I → Q → 0` of presheaves of modules. Project-local: Mathlib has no
173	section Čech complex, hence no short complex of them. -/
174	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexShortComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
175	    (P : ShortComplex X.PresheafOfModules) : ShortComplex (CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ) :=
176	  ShortComplex.mk (sectionCechComplexMap U P.f) (sectionCechComplexMap U P.g) (by
177	    apply HomologicalComplex.hom_ext
178	    intro i
179	    rw [HomologicalComplex.comp_f, HomologicalComplex.zero_f]
180	    show (Limits.Pi.map fun σ : Fin (i + 1) → ι =>
181	        (faceShortComplex U P σ).f) ≫ (Limits.Pi.map fun σ : Fin (i + 1) → ι =>
182	        (faceShortComplex U P σ).g) = 0
183	    rw [Limits.Pi.map_comp_map,
184	      show (fun σ : Fin (i + 1) → ι => (faceShortComplex U P σ).f ≫ (faceShortComplex U P σ).g)
185	        = (fun σ : Fin (i + 1) → ι => (0 : (faceShortComplex U P σ).X₁ ⟶
186	            (faceShortComplex U P σ).X₃)) from by funext σ; exact (faceShortComplex U P σ).zero,
187	      show (Limits.Pi.map fun σ : Fin (i + 1) → ι => (0 : (faceShortComplex U P σ).X₁ ⟶
188	          (faceShortComplex U P σ).X₃)) = 0 from by ext z; simp])
189	
190	/-- **Short exact sequence of section Čech complexes from a basis (Stacks 01EO L1).** Given a
191	short complex `P` of presheaves of modules whose section sequences over every basic open
192	`⨅ₖ U(σ k)` are short exact (left-exactness from the sheaf sequence plus the basis surjectivity
193	of `ses_cech_h1`), the induced section Čech complexes form a short exact sequence. Project-local:
194	this is the term-wise-product step of the 01EO proof; degreewise it is a product of the
195	basic-open section short exact sequences. -/
196	theorem cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
197	    (P : ShortComplex X.PresheafOfModules)
198	    (hface : ∀ (p : ℕ) (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι), (faceShortComplex U P σ).ShortExact) :
199	    (sectionCechComplexShortComplex U P).ShortExact := by
200	  apply HomologicalComplex.shortExact_of_degreewise_shortExact
201	  intro i
202	  exact shortExact_piMap (fun σ : Fin (i + 1) → ι => faceShortComplex U P σ) (hface i)
203	
204	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — L2 homological core (quotient vanishing) -/
205	
206	/-- **Quotient preserves vanishing higher cohomology (homological core, Stacks 01EO L2).**
207	Given a short exact sequence of cochain complexes `0 → X₁ → X₂ → X₃ → 0` in which the middle
208	term `X₂` has vanishing positive homology (the injective/acyclic term) and the left term `X₁`
209	has vanishing positive homology (condition (3)), the right term `X₃` (the quotient) again has
210	vanishing positive homology. Proved by the homology long exact sequence: the connecting map
211	gives `Hᵖ(X₃) ≅ Hᵖ⁺¹(X₁) = 0`. Project-local: the 01EO dimension-shift needs this abstract
212	shape; it is then instantiated at the section Čech complexes. -/
213	theorem cechHomology_quotient_vanishing (T : ShortComplex (CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ))
214	    (hT : T.ShortExact) (hI : ∀ p, 0 < p → IsZero (T.X₂.homology p))
215	    (hF : ∀ p, 0 < p → IsZero (T.X₁.homology p)) :
216	    ∀ p, 0 < p → IsZero (T.X₃.homology p) := by
217	  intro p hp
218	  have hrel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).Rel p (p + 1) := rfl
219	  have hiso : T.X₃.homology p ≅ T.X₁.homology (p + 1) :=
220	    hT.δIso p (p + 1) hrel (hI p hp) (hI (p + 1) (Nat.succ_pos p))
221	  exact (hF (p + 1) (Nat.succ_pos p)).of_iso hiso
222	
223	/-- **Quotient preserves vanishing higher Čech cohomology (Stacks 01EO L2).** Concrete form in
224	terms of `cechCohomology`: with the section Čech short exact sequence of `P` (from L1), if the
225	middle term `I` (injective/acyclic, condition from `injective_cech_acyclic`) and the left term
226	`F` (condition (3)) both have vanishing positive Čech cohomology, then so does the quotient `Q`.
227	Project-local: instantiates `cechHomology_quotient_vanishing` at the section Čech complexes. -/
228	theorem quotient_cech_vanishing_of_basis {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
229	    (P : ShortComplex X.PresheafOfModules)
230	    (hSES : (sectionCechComplexShortComplex U P).ShortExact)
231	    (hI : ∀ p, 0 < p → IsZero (cechCohomology U P.X₂ p))
232	    (hF : ∀ p, 0 < p → IsZero (cechCohomology U P.X₁ p)) :
233	    ∀ p, 0 < p → IsZero (cechCohomology U P.X₃ p) :=
234	  cechHomology_quotient_vanishing (sectionCechComplexShortComplex U P) hSES hI hF
235	
236	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — per-face short exact sequence (01EO) -/
237	
238	/-- The sections functor `Γ(V, -) : X.Modules ⥤ Ab` over a fixed open `V`, as the composite of
239	the inclusion of sheaves of modules into presheaves of modules, the forgetful to presheaves of
240	abelian groups, and evaluation at `V`. Project-local: used only to package the left-exactness of
241	sections (each factor preserves finite limits) for the per-face short exact sequence. -/
242	noncomputable def sectionsFunctor (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : X.Modules ⥤ Ab.{u} :=
243	  Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
244	    (CategoryTheory.evaluation _ _).obj (Opposite.op V)
245	
246	/-- **Per-face short exact sequence from a sheaf short exact sequence (Stacks 01EO).** Given a
247	short exact sequence `S : 0 → F → I → Q → 0` of sheaves of `O_X`-modules and the assumption that
248	on every face `V_σ = ⨅ₖ U(σ k)` the section map `I(V_σ) → Q(V_σ)` is surjective, the per-face
249	short complex `faceShortComplex U (S.map toPresheafOfModules) σ` is short exact. Mono and middle
250	exactness come from left-exactness of the sections functor (`sectionsFunctor` preserves finite
251	limits); the epi is the surjectivity hypothesis (supplied downstream by `ses_cech_h1`).
252	Project-local: produces the per-face hypothesis consumed by `cechComplex_shortExact_of_basis`. -/
253	theorem faceShortComplex_shortExact_of_sheaf_ses {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
254	    (S : ShortComplex X.Modules) (hS : S.ShortExact)
255	    (hsurj : ∀ (p : ℕ) (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι), Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
256	      (faceShortComplex U (S.map (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X)) σ).g))
257	    (p : ℕ) (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
258	    (faceShortComplex U (S.map (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules X)) σ).ShortExact := by
259	  haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (⨅ k, U (σ k))).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
260	    unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
261	  haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (⨅ k, U (σ k))) := by
262	    unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
263	  haveI : Mono S.f := hS.mono_f
264	  change (S.map (sectionsFunctor (⨅ k, U (σ k)))).ShortExact
265	  have hex : (S.map (sectionsFunctor (⨅ k, U (σ k)))).Exact :=
266	    ShortComplex.Exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel hS.exact
267	      (sectionsFunctor (⨅ k, U (σ k))) hS.mono_f inferInstance
268	  haveI : Mono (S.map (sectionsFunctor (⨅ k, U (σ k)))).f :=
269	    inferInstanceAs (Mono ((sectionsFunctor (⨅ k, U (σ k))).map S.f))
270	  haveI : Epi (S.map (sectionsFunctor (⨅ k, U (σ k)))).g := by
271	    rw [AddCommGrpCat.epi_iff_surjective]
272	    exact hsurj p σ
273	  exact ShortComplex.ShortExact.mk hex
