4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafAb
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the structure presheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (1/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	ships the categorical gap-fills, the sheafification / `Ext` typeclass prerequisites,
13	and the per-open ring-map helpers (1)–(5) that culminate in the presheaf-of-
14	`k`-modules `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6). Its sibling `SheafProperty.lean`
15	discharges the sheaf condition and bundles `toModuleKSheaf` (helpers 7–8); the
16	sibling `Carriers.lean` carries the downstream finite-length carriers and producer
17	instances.
18	
19	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
20	-/
21	
22	set_option autoImplicit false
23	
24	universe u v
25	
26	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	universe u₂ v₂
31	
32	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is additive when `D`
33	is preadditive. The `map_add` field reduces to `f + g = f + g` componentwise after
34	`ext; rfl`. -/
35	instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
36	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
37	  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
38	
39	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear when
40	`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
41	load-bearing — bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
42	instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
43	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
44	    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
45	    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
46	  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl
47	
48	namespace Adjunction
49	
50	variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
51	    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
52	    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]
53	
54	include adj
55	
56	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
57	then `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
58	`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
59	default simp lemmas. -/
60	lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
61	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
62	    -- v4.31.0: the old `simp [adj.homEquiv_unit]` no longer closes; the categorical
63	    -- `Linear.comp_smul`/`smul_comp` only match via `erw` (the `≃ₗ` homModule smul is defeq but
64	    -- not syntactically the `Linear.smul` instance), with unit-naturality in between.
65	    simp only [adj.homEquiv_unit, Functor.map_smul, ← Functor.comp_map]
66	    erw [← adj.unit.naturality, Linear.comp_smul, ← adj.unit.naturality, Linear.smul_comp]
67	    rfl)
68	
69	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
70	then `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear. The dual of `left_adjoint_linear`. -/
71	lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
72	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
73	    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])
74	
75	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): linear lifting of `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`. Under
76	`[F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]`, the additive equivalence
77	`(F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃+ (X ⟶ G.obj Y)` upgrades to a `LinearEquiv` over `R`. The
78	`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
79	`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
80	`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
81	noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
82	    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
83	  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
84	  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
85	    map_smul' := fun r f => by
86	      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
87	      simp only [adj.homEquiv_unit, Functor.map_smul]
88	      erw [Linear.comp_smul] }
89	
90	end Adjunction
91	
92	end CategoryTheory
93	
94	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
95	
96	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (a): sheafification on the topology of opens of
97	any topological space, valued in `ModuleCat k`. Inferable from Mathlib's
98	small-site / concrete-category sheafification API; the prover's task is the
99	universe pinning, mirroring the iter-004 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
100	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK
101	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
102	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
103	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
104	  inferInstance
105	
106	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (b): `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe
107	annotation `HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
108	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; mirrors the iter-004
109	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
110	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK
111	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
112	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
113	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
114	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
115	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
116	
117	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
118	
119	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
120	
121	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
122	
123	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
124	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
125	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
126	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
127	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
128	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
129	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
130	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
131	
132	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
133	map of `kToSection`. -/
134	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
135	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
136	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
137	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
140	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
141	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
142	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
143	  rfl
144	
145	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
146	natural in `U`. -/
147	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
148	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
149	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
150	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
151	  rfl
152	
153	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
154	`algebraMap`. -/
155	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
156	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
157	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
158	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
159	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
160	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
161	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
162	
163	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
164	
165	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
166	
167	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
168	
169	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
170	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
171	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
172	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
173	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
174	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
175	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
176	      map_add' := fun x y => by
177	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
178	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
179	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
180	        congr 1
181	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
182	          (C := C) f r }
183	  map_id U := by
184	    ext x
185	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
186	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
187	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
188	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
189	    ext x
190	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
191	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
192	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
193	
194	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
195	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
196	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
197	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
198	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
199	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
200	  rfl
201	
202	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
203

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK.Presheaf
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the sheaf property and bundled structure sheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (2/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	discharges the sheaf condition for `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 7) and bundles the
13	result as `toModuleKSheaf` (helper 8); the forget-and-recover natural iso to the
14	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf is also packaged here. The downstream
15	finite-length carriers live in the sibling `Carriers.lean`.
16	
17	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
18	-/
19	
20	set_option autoImplicit false
21	
22	universe u v
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
27	
28	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
29	
30	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (7): the presheaf of (6) is a sheaf for the
31	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
32	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
33	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
34	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
35	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
36	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
37	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1 <;> rfl
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 5 main: the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme as a sheaf
40	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
41	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
42	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
43	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
44	  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩
45	
46	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
47	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
48	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
49	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`
50	via `forget₂` recovers the iter-004 `toAbSheaf C.left`. Closure body
51	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/
52	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
53	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
54	    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
55	        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
56	      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
57	  Iso.refl _
58	
59	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
60

2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.GenusFiniteness
7	
8	/-!
9	# The last genus-finiteness gates: scoping the Čech-to-derived comparison
10	
11	This file scopes the **final** gates of the adelic genus-finiteness consumable
12	`Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1` (`GenusFiniteness.lean`, node `N12`).
13	That consumable is stated under four gates:
14	
15	1. `HasFiniteMapToP1 C`               — node `N9`  (curve ⟶ ℙ¹, in progress elsewhere);
16	2. `P1HasLaurentChartData k`          — node `N11b` (ℙ¹ chart rings, `P1ChartData.lean`);
17	3. `HasExt.{u}` **and** `HasExt.{u+1}` for the sheaf category
18	   `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`
19	   — the ambient `Ext`-theory the `HModule` genus carrier runs on;
20	4. `∀ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
21	      HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily`
22	   — the Čech-to-derived comparison (Leray/Cartan) on 2-affine covers.
23	
24	The question this file answers: **are gates 3 and 4 instantiable today?**
25	
26	## Gate 3 (the `HasExt` pair): INSTANTIABLE — proved here
27	
28	The genus carrier lives in the sheaf category `𝒮 := Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology
29	C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`, i.e. sheaves of `k`-modules for the *constant* base
30	field `k`.  This is the crucial point that dissolves the historical blocker: `𝒮` is a
31	**Grothendieck abelian category** (Mathlib `IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} (Sheaf J A)` for a
32	small site `J` and Grothendieck-abelian `A = ModuleCat k`), so Mathlib supplies
33	`EnoughInjectives 𝒮` (`079H`) and both `HasExt.{u} 𝒮`, `HasExt.{u+1} 𝒮` (`HasExt.standard`)
34	**for free**.  See `hasExt_moduleKSheaf` / `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf` /
35	`enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf` / `isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf` below.
36	
37	This is *not* the situation of the `SheafOfModules R = X.Modules` apparatus (`Ext` over the
38	structure sheaf `𝒪_X` as a sheaf of rings), which the historical Čech capstone
39	(`CechToCohomology.cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`, `AffineSerreVanishing.affine_serre_vanishing`)
40	runs on: there Mathlib v4.31 provides *no* Grothendieck-abelian / enough-injectives instance
41	for `SheafOfModules`, so every derived-functor statement in that lane carries
42	`[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` / `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` as an un-instantiable
43	hypothesis.  The genus carrier deliberately uses the constant-`k` sheaf category precisely to
44	avoid that trap — and the trap is genuinely avoided.
45	
46	Consequence (`module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`): gate 3 can simply be
47	dropped from the `N12` consumable — Mathlib synthesises it — leaving only gates 1, 2, 4.
48	
49	## Gate 4 (the comparison `∀ S, HasCechToHModuleIso …`): NOT instantiable today
50	
51	`HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰` (`MayerVietorisCover.lean`) is the `Prop`-class packaging of the
52	comparison data `∀ n, cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰)`, i.e. "for the cover
53	`𝒰`, unnormalised Čech cohomology equals derived (`Ext`) cohomology in every degree".
54	
55	For the 2-affine cover `S.coverFamily` this comparison is *mathematically* the Leray statement
56	"an affine cover is acyclic ⟹ Čech computes cohomology", which for a **two**-element cover is
57	exactly the Mayer–Vietoris long exact sequence (`HModule'_sequence_exact`, in-tree) together
58	with **affine Serre vanishing in the constant-`k` sheaf apparatus**:
59	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`, i.e. `Subsingleton (HModule' k
60	(toModuleKSheaf C) i U) = 0` for every affine open `U` and every `i > 0`.  Given that vanishing,
61	the comparison assembles degreewise: `n = 0` (sheaf axiom), `n = 1` (`cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok`
62	= node `N5`, in-tree, plus the MV cokernel identification), `n ≥ 2` (both sides vanish).
63	
64	The **precise gap** is therefore this single named input:
65	
66	> **MISSING:** `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` — Serre vanishing
67	> `Hⁱ(U, 𝒪_C) = 0` (`i > 0`, `U` affine) in the `Sheaf (Opens.gT …) (ModuleCat k)` apparatus,
68	> **plus** the degreewise assembly of the 2-cover comparison from it.
69	
70	Neither piece is a one-line consequence of an existing lemma:
71	
72	* Mathlib has no scheme-cohomology Serre vanishing at all, in any apparatus.
73	* The in-tree Serre vanishing `AffineSerreVanishing.affine_serre_vanishing` lives in the *wrong*
74	  category (`X.Modules`, `Ext (jShriekOU U)`) and is itself gated on the un-instantiable
75	  `[EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules]`; bridging it to the constant-`k` apparatus would need a
76	  "cohomology is independent of the linear structure" comparison that is likewise absent.
77	* The Čech-to-derived carrier `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is currently *produced* in-tree only
78	  from `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` — which itself bundles `HasCechToHModuleIso` on the affine
79	  sub-covers, i.e. the very comparison we are trying to instantiate (a circular producer).
80	
81	So gate 4 is a genuine multi-lemma target, **but the historical infrastructure blocker is gone**:
82	because `EnoughInjectives 𝒮` is now available (proved below), the honest way to close gate 4 is
83	to build affine Serre vanishing + the Čech comparison *directly in the constant-`k` sheaf
84	category* `𝒮` (where injective resolutions exist), rather than attempting to route through the
85	blocked `SheafOfModules` lane.  That is the recommended roadmap for the next wave.
86	
87	## Deliverable of this file
88	
89	* the gate-3 infrastructure, proved and named for citation
90	  (`isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf`, `enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf`,
91	  `hasExt_moduleKSheaf`, `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf`);
92	* the gate-3-free restatement of the `N12` consumable
93	  (`module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`): under gates 1, 2, 4 alone,
94	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` is a finite `k`-module.  Once `P1ChartData` lands (gate 2) and the gate-4
95	  comparison is built, `Module.Finite k H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` — hence the honesty of `genus C` — is
96	  unconditional on a curve with a finite map to ℙ¹.
97	-/
98	
99	universe u
100	
101	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
102	
103	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic
104	
105	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
106	
107	/-! ## Gate 3: the `Ext` infrastructure of the constant-`k` sheaf category is present -/
108	
109	/-- The genus-carrier sheaf category `Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat k)` is **Grothendieck
110	abelian**: sheaves valued in the Grothendieck-abelian `ModuleCat.{u} k` on the small site of
111	opens of `X`.  Mathlib's `IsGrothendieckAbelian.{v} (Sheaf J A)` fires here.  This is the
112	structural fact that makes the derived-functor genus apparatus well-founded (contrast the
113	`SheafOfModules 𝒪_X` apparatus, for which Mathlib v4.31 supplies no such instance). -/
114	theorem isGrothendieckAbelian_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
115	    IsGrothendieckAbelian.{u}
116	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
117	  inferInstance
118	
119	/-- The genus-carrier sheaf category has **enough injectives** (Stacks `079H`, from
120	Grothendieck-abelianness).  This is the input the entire `Ext`/derived-functor comparison
121	needs, and the exact instance the `SheafOfModules 𝒪_X` lane cannot supply.  Its availability
122	here is what dissolves the historical Čech-capstone blocker for the *constant-`k`* apparatus. -/
123	theorem enoughInjectives_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
124	    EnoughInjectives
125	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
126	  inferInstance
127	
128	/-- Gate 3, lower universe: `HasExt.{u}` for the genus-carrier sheaf category is synthesisable
129	(from `HasExt.standard`, itself from Grothendieck-abelianness). -/
130	theorem hasExt_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
131	    HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
132	  inferInstance
133	
134	/-- Gate 3, upper universe: `HasExt.{u+1}` for the genus-carrier sheaf category is
135	synthesisable (the ambient `Ext`-theory the universe-bridged `HModule` genus carrier runs on). -/
136	theorem hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
137	    HasExt.{u+1} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
138	  inferInstance
139	
140	/-! ## Gate-3-free restatement of the genus-finiteness consumable (node `N12`)
141	
142	Because both `HasExt` gates are now synthesised (gate 3 above), the `N12` consumable of
143	`GenusFiniteness.lean` can be restated without them: the only remaining gates are the geometric
144	inputs (`HasFiniteMapToP1 C`, `P1HasLaurentChartData k`) and the Čech-to-derived comparison
145	(gate 4).  This is the honest, tightest current form of adelic genus finiteness. -/
146	
147	/-- **Genus finiteness of the curve, gate-3-free form (node `N12`).**  For a curve `C` over `k`
148	with a finite morphism to ℙ¹ and the ℙ¹ chart data, and with the Čech-to-derived comparison on
149	2-affine covers (gate 4), the genus carrier `H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is a
150	finite `k`-module.  Identical to `Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1` but with the
151	two `HasExt` hypotheses dropped — Mathlib synthesises them from the Grothendieck-abelianness of
152	the constant-`k` sheaf category (`hasExt_moduleKSheaf`, `hasExt_succ_moduleKSheaf`).  Once gates
153	2 and 4 are discharged, `Module.Finite k H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` — and hence the honesty of `genus C` — is
154	unconditional on any curve with a finite map to ℙ¹. -/
155	theorem module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate
156	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [HasFiniteMapToP1 C] [P1HasLaurentChartData k]
157	    [∀ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
158	      HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily] :
159	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
160	  module_finite_hModule_one_of_finite_map C
161	    (fun S => ⟨S.hModuleOneEquivH1Cok (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)⟩)
162	
163	end AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.RiemannRoch.Adelic.CechComparisonGate
7	
8	/-!
9	# Gate 4: the Čech-to-derived comparison on 2-affine covers, reduced to affine vanishing
10	
11	This file attacks **gate 4** of the unconditional genus-finiteness consumable
12	`Adelic.module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate`
13	(`CechComparisonGate.lean`), namely the instance
14	
15	```
16	∀ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily.
17	```
18	
19	`HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰` (`MayerVietorisCover.lean`) packages, for the cover `𝒰`, a
20	degreewise `k`-linear comparison
21	`∀ n, cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰)` between unnormalised Čech
22	cohomology and the `Ext`-based derived cohomology `HModule'`.
23	
24	## The mathematical reduction (Leray on a 2-affine cover)
25	
26	For the 2-affine cover `S.coverFamily` of a separated `k`-curve `C`, with the three
27	pieces `U₁`, `U₂`, `U₁ ⊓ U₂` affine (the `AffineCoverMVSquare` fields), the comparison
28	is the classical statement "an acyclic cover computes derived cohomology", which for a
29	**two**-element cover is the Mayer–Vietoris long exact sequence
30	(`HModule'_sequence_exact`, in-tree) fed by **affine Serre vanishing in the constant-`k`
31	sheaf apparatus** `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`, i.e.
32	`Subsingleton (HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) i U) = 0` for every affine open `U` and
33	every `i > 0`.  Given that vanishing the comparison assembles degreewise:
34	
35	* `n = 0`: sheaf axiom (`H⁰` on both sides is the group of global sections);
36	* `n = 1`: `AffineCoverMVSquare.cechCohomologyOneEquivH1Cok` (node N5, in-tree,
37	  unconditional) `cechCohomology C F S.coverFamily 1 ≃ₗ[k] H1Cok S F`, chained with the
38	  MV cokernel identification `H1Cok S F ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F 1 ⊤` (which uses the
39	  degree-`0`/degree-`1` slice of the MV LES together with the affine `H¹`-vanishing on
40	  `U₁`, `U₂`);
41	* `n ≥ 2`: both sides vanish — the Čech complex of a 2-element cover is `3`-term, and the
42	  derived side vanishes by the MV LES together with the affine vanishing on
43	  `U₁`, `U₂`, `U₁ ⊓ U₂`.
44	
45	## What this file establishes
46	
47	* `AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_coverFamily` / `isAffineOpen_overlapOpen`: the three
48	  pieces of the cover, in their `coverFamily`/`overlapOpen` typed forms, are affine
49	  (bridging the structure fields to the family-indexed forms the degreewise assembly
50	  consumes).
51	* `AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_coverFamily` /
52	  `subsingleton_hModule'_overlapOpen`: the **per-piece derived vanishing** — under
53	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F`, `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i (piece))` for every
54	  `i > 0`.  These are the affine-vanishing inputs the Mayer–Vietoris degreewise assembly
55	  reads off (the `H¹` cokernel identification and the `n ≥ 2` whole-space vanishing are
56	  both immediate consequences of the MV LES fed by exactly these).
57	* `module_finite_hModule_one_unconditional`: the capstone, stated under the honest gate
58	  set (finite map to ℙ¹, ℙ¹ chart data, and the gate-4 comparison), delivering
59	  `Module.Finite k (H¹(C, 𝒪_C))`.  This is `module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1`
60	  with the `HasExt` gates synthesised (via `CechComparisonGate`); once the gate-4 instance
61	  is supplied — i.e. once `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` is proved and
62	  the degreewise assembly built — the genus of the curve is honest.
63	
64	## The precise residual
65	
66	The single named residual for the **unconditional** gate 4 is
67	
68	> `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` — affine Serre vanishing
69	> `Hⁱ(U, 𝒪_C) = 0` (`i > 0`, `U` affine) in the `Sheaf (Opens.gT …) (ModuleCat k)`
70	> apparatus —
71	
72	together with the degreewise assembly (the degree-`0`/degree-`1` MV cokernel
73	identification and the `n ≥ 2` whole-space vanishing) built from it.  No `sorry`/axiom or
74	sorried instance is introduced here; the residual is the existing `Prop`-class
75	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`, whose producer for the structure sheaf is the genuine open
76	target (Mathlib supplies no scheme-cohomology Serre vanishing in this apparatus, and the
77	in-tree `X.Modules` vanishing is gated on the un-instantiable
78	`[EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules]`).
79	
80	**Universe note for the assembly (probe-confirmed here).**  The affine vanishing lives at
81	`HModule' = Abelian.Ext.{u}` (`Type u`), but the in-tree Mayer–Vietoris LES
82	`AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence` has its `HasExt` baked at `Abelian.Ext.{u+1}`
83	(`Type u+1`): the two carriers are *not* definitionally equal, so the degreewise assembly
84	must transport the per-piece vanishing across the universe bridge
85	`Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv` (`MayerVietorisCore.lean`) before feeding it into the LES.
86	This is the same `Ext.{u}` ↔ `Ext.{u+1}` bridge the `HModule'`/`HModule` interface already
87	uses (`HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`), and is the one non-obvious wrinkle a future
88	author of the assembly must account for.
89	-/
90	
91	set_option autoImplicit false
92	
93	universe u
94	
95	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
96	
97	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
98	
99	section Affineness
100	
101	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {X : Scheme.{u}} (S : X.AffineCoverMVSquare)
102	
103	/-- Each piece of the 2-element cover family is affine: `S.coverFamily i` is `U₁` or
104	`U₂`, both affine by the `AffineCoverMVSquare` fields. -/
105	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_coverFamily (i : ULift.{u} (Fin 2)) :
106	    IsAffineOpen (S.coverFamily i) := by
107	  rcases i with ⟨i⟩
108	  fin_cases i
109	  · exact S.isAffineOpen_U₁
110	  · exact S.isAffineOpen_U₂
111	
112	/-- The overlap `U₁ ⊓ U₂` of the 2-element cover, in the `overlapOpen` typed form used by
113	the degreewise Čech assembly, is affine. -/
114	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.isAffineOpen_overlapOpen :
115	    IsAffineOpen S.overlapOpen :=
116	  S.isAffineOpen_inf
117	
118	end Affineness
119	
120	/-! ## Per-piece derived-cohomology vanishing (the acyclicity inputs of the assembly)
121	
122	Under affine Serre vanishing `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F`, each affine piece of the
123	2-affine cover has vanishing higher derived cohomology `HModule'`.  These are the exact
124	inputs the degreewise Čech comparison consumes (the Mayer–Vietoris LES on the cover reads
125	off `H¹(⊤)` and the higher vanishing from precisely these). -/
126	
127	section PieceVanishing
128	
129	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))}
130	  (S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare)
131	  (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
132	  [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
133	  [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
134	
135	/-- Higher derived cohomology of `F` vanishes on each piece `S.coverFamily j` of the
136	2-affine cover (both pieces are affine). -/
137	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_coverFamily
138	    (j : ULift.{u} (Fin 2)) (i : ℕ) (hi : 0 < i) :
139	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i (S.coverFamily j)) :=
140	  IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) (S.isAffineOpen_coverFamily j) i hi
141	
142	/-- Higher derived cohomology of `F` vanishes on the overlap `U₁ ⊓ U₂` of the 2-affine
143	cover (the overlap is affine). -/
144	lemma AffineCoverMVSquare.subsingleton_hModule'_overlapOpen
145	    (i : ℕ) (hi : 0 < i) :
146	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i S.overlapOpen) :=
147	  IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) S.isAffineOpen_overlapOpen i hi
148	
149	end PieceVanishing
150	
151	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
152	
153	/-! ## The capstone: genus finiteness under the honest gate set
154	
155	Once the gate-4 comparison instance
156	`∀ S, HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily` is available (its unconditional
157	production is the residual `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` plus the
158	degreewise assembly), the genus carrier `H¹(C, 𝒪_C)` is a finite `k`-module. -/
159	
160	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic
161	
162	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
163	
164	/-- **Genus finiteness of the curve, honest gate-set form.**  For a curve `C` over `k`
165	with a finite morphism to ℙ¹ (`HasFiniteMapToP1`), the ℙ¹ chart data
166	(`P1HasLaurentChartData`), and the Čech-to-derived comparison on 2-affine covers (gate 4),
167	the genus carrier `H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1` is a finite `k`-module.
168	
169	This is `module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate` (the `HasExt`-free
170	restatement, `CechComparisonGate.lean`) named as the lane capstone.  It becomes
171	**unconditional** exactly when gate 2 (`P1HasLaurentChartData k`, lane A) and gate 4
172	(this file's target instance, whose residual is affine Serre vanishing
173	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`) are both discharged. -/
174	theorem module_finite_hModule_one_unconditional
175	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) [HasFiniteMapToP1 C] [P1HasLaurentChartData k]
176	    [∀ S : C.left.AffineCoverMVSquare,
177	      HasCechToHModuleIso (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) S.coverFamily] :
178	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1) :=
179	  module_finite_hModule_one_of_finiteMapToP1_of_cechGate C
180	
181	end AlgebraicGeometry.Adelic

1	# Čech / Koszul / acyclic-cover precedent audit (iter-059)
2	
3	## Project context
4	
5	Iter-053–058 set up the infrastructure for a forthcoming Čech-acyclicity
6	proof on basic-open covers:
7	
8	- `class HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F` (iter-053) — for every affine `U`, the
9	  existence of a cover `𝒰` of `U` such that `[IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]` and
10	  `[HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰]`.
11	- `basicOpenCover s : s → Opens` and its supremum / membership /
12	  pairwise- / Finset-`inf'`-intersection identifications
13	  (iter-054 → iter-058).
14	- `class IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰` (iter-048) — positive-degree
15	  Čech cohomology subsingleton.
16	- `class HasCechToHModuleIso F 𝒰` (iter-050) — `Nonempty`-wrapped
17	  `∀ n, cechCohomology n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆𝒰)`.
18	
19	The project now needs, for `F := toModuleKSheaf C`,
20	
21	1. `IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)` whenever
22	   `Ideal.span (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) = ⊤`.
23	2. `HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)` for the same
24	   `s`.
25	
26	Before committing iter-059+ to the project-local construction, this analogy
27	call audits Mathlib for prior art.
28	
29	## What `cechComplexFunctor` produces
30	
31	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:65`
32	
33	```lean
34	noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
35	  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech
36	```
37	
38	Unfolding the constituents:
39	
40	- `FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor E = cosimplicialObjectFunctor E
41	  ⋙ AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A`
42	  (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:53`)
43	- `cosimplicialObjectFunctor E = evalOp C A ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj
44	  E.rightOp` (same file, L43)
45	- `(FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech.obj ⦋n⦌ = U.power (Fin (n+1))`, whose
46	  underlying object in `C` is `∏ᶜ (U ∘ i)` indexed by
47	  `i : Fin (n + 1) → ι` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/Cech.lean:34,186`)
48	
49	In our setting `C = (Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ`, `A = ModuleCat k`,
50	`P = (sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F`, finite products in `Opens` are infima.
51	The cochain at degree `n` is therefore exactly
52	
53	```
54	∏_{x : Fin (n+1) → ι} F(⨅ k : Fin (n+1), 𝒰 (x k))
55	```
56	
57	— a `Module k` product (not a Π-type) inside `ModuleCat.{u} k`.
58	The differential `d^n` is the standard alternating sum
59	`∑_{j : Fin (n+2)} (-1)^j · δ_j` (Mathlib's `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`,
60	`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/AlternatingFaceMapComplex.lean:67`).
61	
62	**Crucial caveat**: this is the *unaugmented* Čech complex. There is no
63	`F(U) → ∏_i F(𝒰 i)` term sitting in degree `-1`. Standard Čech-acyclicity
64	for affine covers is usually stated about the *augmented* complex (Stacks
65	01EJ / 03B1), so the project either has to build the augmentation
66	explicitly, or carry the equivalent reasoning at the level of the
67	unaugmented homology (using the sheaf axiom for degree 0).
68	
69	## What Mathlib has
70	
71	### Precedent: `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (definition only)
72	
73	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean:65` — file path
74	**confirmed unchanged** since iter-047; the import statement in
75	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean:784,796,813`
76	still resolves.
77	
78	```lean
79	noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ
80	```
81	
82	Used by iter-012 / iter-047 to define `cechCochain_OC`, `cechCochain`,
83	`cechCohomology`. No comparison theorem to derived functors is bundled
84	with the construction.
85	
86	### Precedent: `CategoryTheory.SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
87	
88	`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:328`
89	
90	```lean
91	noncomputable def homotopyEquiv [Preadditive C] [HasZeroObject C]
92	    {X : SimplicialObject.Augmented C} (ed : ExtraDegeneracy X) :
93	    HomotopyEquiv (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.obj (drop.obj X))
94	      ((ChainComplex.single₀ C).obj (point.obj X))
95	```
96	
97	Augmented-simplicial-with-extra-degeneracy ⇒ alternating face map complex
98	is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on the augmentation. This
99	is the **engine** for proving that a Čech complex over a "good" cover is
100	null-homotopic in positive degrees: the work that would otherwise be the
101	alternating-sum-cancellation argument is encapsulated here. Note this is
102	on `AlternatingFaceMapComplex` (chain complex) rather than
103	`alternatingCofaceMapComplex` (cochain complex) — a dual will need to be
104	spelled out (or extracted by op).
105	
106	### Precedent: `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
107	
108	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean:92`
109	
110	```lean
111	noncomputable def extraDegeneracyCech {i₀ : U.I} (d : T ⟶ U.obj i₀) :
112	    (U.cech.augmentOfIsTerminal (isTerminalIncl _ hT)).ExtraDegeneracy
113	```
114	
115	Specialises `homotopyEquiv` to the formal-coproduct Čech object: given a
116	"vertex" — a section `T ⟶ U.obj i₀` of one of the cover terms from the
117	terminal — the augmented Čech object has an extra degeneracy. **This is
118	what fires after localizing at a single `f_i`**: in `D(f_i)`, the basic
119	open `D(f_i)` is a unit, so the affine `Spec(R[1/f_i]) = D(f_i)` is the
120	terminal of the cover-of-`D(f_i)` slice, and the inclusion `D(f_i) ⊂
121	D(f_i)` (i.e. the cover's `i₀ = i` member) supplies the section. Modulo
122	op-direction bookkeeping, this gives "Čech complex over `D(f_i)` is
123	null-homotopic in positive degrees" almost for free.
124	
125	### Precedent: `exact_of_isLocalized_span`
126	
127	`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean:173`
128	
129	```lean
130	lemma exact_of_isLocalized_span (H : ∀ r : s, Function.Exact
131	    (map (.powers r.1) (f r) (g r) F) (map (.powers r.1) (g r) (h r) G)) :
132	    Function.Exact F G
133	```
134	
135	(plus its sibling at L211, `exact_of_localized_span`, which directly
136	takes `f : M →ₗ N` without prequalifying localization data.)
137	
138	Hypothesis shape: for each generator `r ∈ s` with `span s = ⊤`,
139	`map (.powers r) (f r) (g r) F` (the induced map between localizations)
140	is composed with `map (.powers r) (g r) (h r) G` to be exact. Conclusion:
141	`F` and `G` are exact unlocalized.
142	
143	**This is the spanning-set Koszul-style local exactness lemma**. The
144	iter-057 correction (noting the project's iter-056 docstring referenced a
145	non-existent `exact_of_localized_span` without the "is" prefix) was an
146	overcorrection: both `exact_of_localized_span` and
147	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` exist; the former is the user-facing direct
148	form, the latter is the explicit-localization-data form. The iter-058
149	report should clarify both forms exist and which one the iter-059+ work
150	will actually call.

152	### Precedent: `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`

154	`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean:56`

157	theorem IsLocalizedModule.map_exact (g : M₀ →ₗ[R] M₁) (h : M₁ →ₗ[R] M₂)
158	    (ex : Function.Exact g h) :
159	    Function.Exact (map S f₀ f₁ g) (map S f₁ f₂ h)

161	
162	Localization preserves exactness chain-level. Used as a sub-lemma in
163	`exact_of_isLocalized_span`, but on its own it's the converse direction
164	(unlocalized exact ⇒ localized exact), which is what we need to *transit*
165	from "all localizations exact" to "complex is exact at each `D(f_i)`":
166	the project will need `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` if it wants to

238	
239	### Absent precedent: Koszul complex / Koszul resolution
240	
241	**Mathlib has no `KoszulComplex` file** (search:
242	`Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/KoszulComplex.lean`, `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/Koszul/`,
243	`*Koszul*` glob — none). The project must either build a Koszul-complex
244	formalisation (large) **or** go via the localization-at-each-`f` route
245	described below — which is what the standard pre-Koszul proof of Čech
246	acyclicity uses anyway.
247	
248	## Cited references
249	
250	### Stacks Project §01ED (Čech cohomology)
251	
252	The acyclicity-on-affine proof in the Stacks Project (tag 01ED, sections
253	on Čech cohomology of quasicoherent sheaves on affine schemes) goes as
254	follows, *without* invoking Koszul:
255	
256	1. For an affine `U = Spec R`, a finite cover `𝒰 = (D(f_i))_i` with
257	   `(f_i)` generating the unit ideal of `R`, and a quasi-coherent `F`
258	   with `M = F(U)`, the augmented Čech complex
259	   `0 → M → ∏_i M[1/f_i] → ∏_{ij} M[1/f_i f_j] → ...` is exact.
260	
261	2. **Proof**: exactness is local on `D(f_i)` for each `i`. Localising at
262	   `f_i`, the augmented Čech complex over `D(f_i)` has `D(f_i)` as a cover
263	   member (`f_i` is a unit in `R[1/f_i]`), giving an extra degeneracy
264	   via "inclusion of the vertex". By the standard extra-degeneracy
265	   homotopy, the localised augmented complex is contractible, hence
266	   exact.
267	
268	3. By "exact on each open of a spanning cover ⇒ exact globally" (which
269	   is `exact_of_isLocalized_span` in Mathlib), the original augmented
270	   complex is exact.
271	
272	This route is the most economical for our setting: it requires *no*
273	Koszul-complex machinery; Mathlib's `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
274	encapsulates the alternating-sum cancellation; and the local-to-global
275	step is exactly `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
276	
277	### Stacks Project §03OU (Čech-to-cohomology spectral sequence)
278	
279	The general comparison `H^p(C^•(𝒰, F)) → H^p(U, F)` (Čech-to-derived
280	edge map) extends to a spectral sequence
281	`E^{p,q}_2 = Ȟ^p(𝒰, H^q(F)) ⇒ H^{p+q}(U, F)`. When the cover is acyclic
282	(all `H^q(intersection, F) = 0` for `q > 0`), the spectral sequence
283	degenerates and the edge map is an isomorphism. Mathlib does not have
284	this spectral sequence in any form. **For the project, the most
285	economical substitute** is to construct the edge map directly (without
286	the spectral sequence) by:
287	
288	- Producing the augmented Čech complex `C^• : 0 → F(U) → C^0 → C^1 → ...`
289	  on the affine. By step (1)–(3) above, this is acyclic, hence a
290	  resolution of `F(U)`.
291	- Sheaf-evaluating against an injective resolution of `F` (Mathlib does
292	  not provide injective sheaves in general, so this is the bottleneck).
293	- Identifying the resulting double-complex bipartite homology with
294	  `Ext`.
295	
296	Honest Lean blueprint estimate for the comparison: ~200-400 LOC.
297	
298	## Comparison with the project
299	
300	### What the project currently has
301	
302	- `Scheme.cechCochain` (iter-047): `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj
303	  ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)` — the unaugmented cochain complex,
304	  matching Mathlib's API.
305	- `Scheme.cechCohomology` (iter-047): `(Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n`.
306	- iter-054→058 basic-open identifications: `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`,
307	  `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`, `..._inter_eq_basicOpen_mul`,
308	  `..._finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`, `..._finset_inf'_isAffineOpen`,
309	  `..._finset_inf'_le`. All thin Mathlib wrappers, all needed.
310	- `HModule k F n` and `HModule' k F n X` as `Abelian.Ext` against the
311	  constant sheaf.
312	
313	### What the project must provide
314	
315	#### Branch A — Čech acyclicity (~150-250 LOC)
316	
317	For each affine `U` and spanning `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`:
318	
319	1. **Augmentation lemma** (~30-50 LOC). Define the augmentation
320	   `F(U) → ∏_i F(𝒰 i)` as the natural sheaf-restriction morphism, and
321	   produce an *augmented* cochain complex extending iter-047's
322	   `Scheme.cechCochain`. This is structural; no Mathlib wrapper exists.
323	
324	2. **Localized identification** (~60-100 LOC). For each `f ∈ s`, identify
325	   the localization (at `f`) of the degree-`n` Čech cochain
326	   `∏_x F(⨅ k, 𝒰 (x k))` with the degree-`n` cochain of the
327	   "cover-of-`D(f)`" Čech complex. The key inputs are
328	   `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (each intersection
329	   identifies as `Localization.Away` of `Γ(U)`) and
330	   `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (chain-level localization-preserves-exactness).
331	
332	3. **Extra-degeneracy invocation** (~30-60 LOC). The cover-of-`D(f)`
333	   Čech complex contains `D(f) = D(f ∩ f)` as a member, so applying
334	   `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` (or its op-dual for cochain
335	   complexes) gives an extra degeneracy on the augmented complex. By
336	   `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`, the augmented cochain complex over
337	   `D(f)` is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on `F(D(f))`,
338	   hence the (un-augmented) Čech complex over `D(f)` is exact in
339	   positive degrees.
340	
341	4. **Local-to-global** (~30-40 LOC). Per-degree, apply
342	   `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ...` with the per-`f` local exactness
343	   from step 3 to lift to exactness of the unlocalized differential.
344	   Repackage as `IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)`.
345	
346	#### Branch B — Čech-vs-derived comparison (~150-200 LOC)
347	
348	This is the substantial branch. Possibilities, ranked by economy:
349	
350	(i) **Direct comparison via augmented complex as resolution** (~150-200
351	LOC). After Branch A, the augmented Čech complex is exact, hence a
352	resolution of `F(U)`. Use `Abelian.Ext`'s functoriality (Mathlib has
353	`Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀` and adjacent API) to identify the homology of
354	`Hom(augmented Čech, F)` with `Ext^n(R, F)`.
355	
356	(ii) **Inductive Mayer-Vietoris** (~250-400 LOC). For finite covers,
357	unwind the cover one basic open at a time using the iter-014 LES.
358	Requires inductive structure and ad-hoc finite-cover reduction. **Not
359	recommended** — does more work and produces a less reusable artefact.
360	
361	(iii) **Spectral sequence formalisation** (~600+ LOC). Build Stacks 03OU
362	in Lean. **Strongly not recommended** — out of scope for the genus
363	proof.
364	
365	**Recommendation**: pursue (i). Total Branch B estimate ~150-200 LOC.
366	
367	### Total project-local LOC estimate
368	
369	~300-450 LOC across Branches A + B, spanning iter-059 to iter-066+.
370	This is within the directive's "~350-650 LOC" upper bound — the
371	mathlib-faithful design comes in at the low end of the plan-agent's
372	estimate.
373	
374	## Recommendation
375	
376	**Do not bypass the Čech-vs-derived comparison.** No Mathlib precedent
377	exists for "acyclic cover ⇒ Čech = Ext", but the constituent pieces —
378	`ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`, `extraDegeneracyCech`,
379	`exact_of_isLocalized_span`, `isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` — combine
380	into the standard Stacks 01ED proof of Čech acyclicity (Branch A) with
381	modest project-local glue (~150-250 LOC). The Čech-vs-derived comparison
382	(Branch B) is the real obligation; spending ~150-200 LOC on the direct
383	"augmented Čech complex is a resolution" route gives the cleanest
384	deliverable.
385	
386	**The alternating-sum-cancellation argument the directive feared having
387	to reproduce is NOT needed**: `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` encapsulates
388	it. The project work is the *bookkeeping* of mapping the localized Čech
389	complex to a Čech complex over `D(f)` (Branch A step 2), not the cancellation
390	itself.
391	
392	**Naming alignment**: all paths and names cited by the project (iter-047
393	import, iter-053–058 wrappers) remain valid. Recommend the iter-059
394	plan-agent update the iter-056 docstring at
395	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean:1441` to say
396	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (or `exact_of_localized_span`, depending on
397	which form the project ends up calling — both exist).
398	
399	## Caveats
400	
401	- **Op direction**: Mathlib's `extraDegeneracyCech` builds on
402	  `SimplicialObject.Augmented`, giving an augmented *simplicial* object
403	  with extra degeneracy. The corresponding alternating face map complex
404	  is a *chain* complex. Our Čech complex is a *cochain* complex (built
405	  from an *augmented cosimplicial* object via
406	  `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). The op-duality is straightforward but
407	  takes ~20-30 LOC of routine repackaging.
408	- **Augmentation construction**: the augmentation `F(U) → ∏_i F(𝒰 i)` is
409	  natural in `F`, so it should be derivable from the universal property
410	  of the formal-coproduct construction. But the project will need to
411	  spell this out — Mathlib does not currently package the augmentation
412	  alongside `cechComplexFunctor`.
413	- **Quasi-coherence assumption**: Stacks 01ED is for *quasi-coherent*
414	  sheaves on affine schemes, where the augmented Čech complex is exact
415	  *as a complex of `Γ(U)`-modules*. Our `toModuleKSheaf` is not the
416	  structure sheaf as an `O_C`-module — it's the structure sheaf as a
417	  *constant `k`-module*. The Stacks proof carries over because the
418	  alternating-sum cancellation is purely *combinatorial* (lives at the
419	  level of the simplicial structure, not the algebra structure). But the
420	  blueprint should note this distinction so future readers don't
421	  conflate "quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_C`-modules" with "sheaves of
422	  `k`-modules underlying the structure sheaf".
423	- **`Subsingleton` vs `IsZero`**: project's `IsCechAcyclicCover` carries
424	  a `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` field. Mathlib's
425	  `subsingleton_H_of_isZero` (`SheafCohomology/Basic.lean:74`) reduces
426	  via `IsZero`. The project may want to expose both forms; the
427	  `Subsingleton` form is the chainable one (matches iter-040's
428	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` consumer).
429	
430	## Other open questions noticed (not addressed)
431	
432	- Whether the iter-046 `Functor.const_linear` / `Functor.const_additive`
433	  instances suffice for the `Linear k`-enrichment on `Sheaf J (ModuleCat
434	  k)` that Branch B will need (probably yes, but worth a separate sanity
435	  check).
436	- Whether the iter-009 `noncomputable abbrev HModule` / iter-014
437	  `HModule'` design needs adjustment when the Čech-vs-Ext comparison

439	  universe bridge already handles the `u`-vs-`u+1` step; whether it
440	  needs to handle the augmentation universe too is a separate audit.

42	## Decisions identified
43	
44	### Decision Q1a: product-localisation commutation `IsLocalizedModule.pi`
45	
46	- **Mathlib idiom**: `IsLocalizedModule.pi`. Cite:
47	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi:93`.
48	
49	  ```lean
50	  instance pi {ι : Type*} [Finite ι]
51	      {M M' : ι → Type*} [∀ i, AddCommMonoid (M i)] [∀ i, AddCommMonoid (M' i)]
52	      [∀ i, Module R (M i)] [∀ i, Module R (M' i)]
53	      (f : ∀ i, M i →ₗ[R] M' i) [∀ i, IsLocalizedModule S (f i)] :
54	      IsLocalizedModule S (.pi fun i ↦ f i ∘ₗ .proj i)
55	  ```
56	
57	  Plus the binary sibling `IsLocalizedModule.prodMap` at L77 of the
58	  same file. Both are derived from
59	  `IsBaseChange.pi` / `IsBaseChange.prodMap` via the
60	  `isLocalizedModule_iff_isBaseChange` adapter, which means the
61	  underlying `IsLocalizedModule (LinearMap.pi …)` instance is
62	  definitionally a base-change statement — exactly the universal
63	  property "localisation commutes with finite products".
64	
65	  Why Mathlib chose this: localisation = base change against
66	  `Localization S`; base change of modules commutes with finite limits
67	  in `ModuleCat R` (a categorical fact); finite products in `ModuleCat
68	  R` are the dependent `∀ i, M' i`-types via `ModuleCat.piIsoPi`. The
69	  instance is a typeclass so consumers don't have to invoke it by hand.
70	
71	- **Project's current path**: the iter-069 docstring at
72	  `BasicOpenCech.lean:1583` describes "the
73	  product-localisation commutation `LocalizedModule (powers f) (∏ᶜ_x M_x) ≅
74	  ∏ᶜ_x LocalizedModule (powers f) M_x` holds". The sorry at L1783
75	  (`h_loc_exact`) is supposed to be closed by this commutation plus
76	  `h_a₀_fun f`. As of iter-106 no call site has been written.
77	
78	- **Gap**: identical (modulo `LinearMap.pi`-vs-`Pi.lift` repackaging).
79	  The Mathlib instance covers the `LinearMap.pi`-flavoured product;
80	  bridging to `Limits.Pi.lift`-flavoured product in `ModuleCat k`
81	  proceeds via `ModuleCat.piIsoPi : (∏ᶜ Z) ≅ ModuleCat.of k (∀ i, Z i)`
82	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Products`), which the project
83	  already uses (the `e₁`, `e₂`, `e₃` `LinearEquiv` handles at

92	### Decision Q1b: per-coord localisation premise

101	     (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.IsLocalization`) upgrades
102	     an `IsLocalization` algebra map to an `IsLocalizedModule` instance

109	  by working with `LocalizedModule` directly rather than going via the
110	  slice cover. This means the project has bypassed the natural
111	  per-coord identification with `Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1))`.
112	
113	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. The current project path sidesteps the
114	  slice-cover identification, so `h_loc_exact`'s closure cannot
115	  consume `h_a₀_fun f` directly — there's no Mathlib lemma
116	  ``IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_pi → LocalizedModule.map (...) f_R``
117	  that bypasses the slice-cover identification.
118	
119	  The bridge has to go: `slice-cover.f at coord n` (R-linear in
120	  `ModuleCat k`) = `LinearMap.pi (per-coord restriction)` ∘
121	  `K₀.f at coord n` (under suitable identification), then identify
122	  `LocalizedModule.map (powers f.1) f_R` with the LHS via
123	  `IsLocalizedModule.iso` and uniqueness.
124	
125	- **Cost of divergence**: zero new Mathlib gap, but ~50 LOC of project
126	  glue to build the per-coord algebra structures, install the
127	  `IsLocalization.Away` instances on the slice factors, and bridge to
128	  the `IsLocalizedModule` form via the Mathlib adapter.
129	
130	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB (the iter-069 docstring already
131	  outlines this; the project just needs to write it).
132	
133	### Decision Q1c: transporting `Function.Exact` across the localisation iso
134	
135	- **Mathlib idiom**: `IsLocalizedModule.iso`
136	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic`) gives
137	  `LocalizedModule S M ≃ₗ[R] M'` whenever `IsLocalizedModule S f`. The
138	  pair `IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext` / `IsLocalizedModule.ext`
139	  provides extensionality. For `Function.Exact` transport across a
140	  `LinearEquiv`, `LinearEquiv.exact_iff_exact` (search: `LinearEquiv +
141	  Function.Exact`) is the canonical move.
142	
143	- **Project's current path**: not yet written; the sorry at L1783 is
144	  raw.
145	
146	- **Gap**: identical to Mathlib's intended use. No divergence.
147	
148	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (no Mathlib precedent dictates the exact glue
149	  shape; the project just writes it).
150	
151	### Decision Q2a: post-hoc R-linearity certification
152	
153	- **Mathlib idiom**: the canonical idiom in Mathlib is to **build the
154	  complex in the right category in the first place** rather than
155	  certify R-linearity post hoc. `cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤
156	  CochainComplex A ℕ` (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafCohomology.Cech:65`)
157	  works for any `A` with the requisite limits — in particular for
158	  `A := ModuleCat R`, giving a Čech complex of **R-modules** whose
159	  differentials are R-linear by construction.
160	
161	  `CategoryTheory.Linear R (ModuleCat R)` is canonical: hom-sets in
162	  `ModuleCat R` ARE the R-modules of R-linear maps; composition and
163	  addition commute with the R-action by definition.
164	
165	  But `Linear R (ModuleCat k)` does **NOT** hold for `R ≠ k` in
166	  general: a `k`-linear map between `R`-modules-over-`k` need not be
167	  R-linear. There is no `Linear R`-instance on `ModuleCat k`, nor any
168	  Mathlib lemma "alternating sum of R-linear maps in
169	  `Hom_{ModuleCat k}(M, N)` is R-linear", because the hom-set isn't an
170	  R-module to start with.
171	
172	  `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex` lives over
173	  `Preadditive C` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex`)
174	  and inherits nothing more — it neither requires nor exposes a
175	  `Linear R`-structure.
176	
177	- **Project's current path**: build `cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰`
178	  in `CochainComplex (ModuleCat k) ℕ` and prove R-linearity of the
179	  differential post hoc via the iter-099+ alternating-sum scaffolding
180	  (`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux`, `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`,
181	  `alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`, the named family
182	  `cechCofaceMap_summand_family` + its R-linearity at iter-102/104).
183	
184	  The 7-iter-stuck blocker at L1120 is *bridging* the iter-104
185	  binder-level R-linearity proof to the call-site anonymous-closure
186	  form of the K₀ differential.
187	
188	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. Mathlib's idiom is "build in
189	  `ModuleCat R` if you need R-linearity"; the project's `ModuleCat k`
190	  + post-hoc-certify path has produced 7 consecutive failed iters
191	  because the categorical infrastructure (`Pi.lift`, alternating sum,
192	  `eqToHom`) is precisely tuned to preserve `Preadditive`/`Linear k`
193	  structure but obstinately whnf-reduces anonymous closures in ways
194	  that defeat the elaborator when tactics try to extract per-summand
195	  R-linearity.
196	
197	  The cost is *exactly* the 7 stuck iters: post-hoc R-linearity
198	  certification on a `ModuleCat k`-valued Čech differential is a
199	  Mathlib gap **created by the project's design choice**, not a
200	  pre-existing Mathlib gap that needs filling.
201	
202	- **Verdict**: ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB at the architectural level. Two
203	  feasible paths (see Recommendation below).
204	
205	### Decision Q2b: tactical bridging via `change` to named family
206	
207	- **Mathlib idiom**: no Mathlib precedent for this micro-level
208	  reformulation (Mathlib doesn't run into it because of Q2a's
209	  architectural choice).
210	
211	- **Project's current path**: the iter-099 attack route through
212	  `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`/`alternating_zsmul_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`
213	  tries to apply the structural lemma at the *call site* via
214	  Miller-pattern unification, with `G := fun i ↦ (anonymous closure)`
215	  baked into the `?G` placeholder. The unifier fails because the
216	  anonymous closure under whnf has nested `Pi.lift` bodies that the
217	  elaborator cannot decompose.
218	
219	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (in the sense that the project keeps
220	  pursuing this route despite consistent failure).
221	
222	- **Cost**: 7 consecutive stuck iters and counting.
223	
224	- **Verdict**: PROCEED only if a fundamentally different micro-tactic
225	  (e.g. body-level `set F := cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n` followed
226	  by `change ... = ∑ i ∈ Finset.univ, (-1)^↑i • F i ≫ eqToHom ⋯` at the
227	  *top* of the body — before any of the unfolds at L998-L1007 — so
228	  that the named family is what the unfolds reduce against) is
229	  attempted. **If this also fails, fall back to Q2a Path A
230	  (architectural refactor)**.
231	
232	## Recommendation
233	
234	### For Q1 (`h_loc_exact` sorry at BasicOpenCech.lean:1783)
235	
236	**ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB**. The closure recipe is:
237	
238	```
239	1. For each f ∈ s₀ and each x : Fin (n+1) → ↑s₀:
240	   a. V_x := ∏ᶜ_a basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (x a) is affine
241	      (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen, iter-057).
242	   b. V_x ⊓ D(f.1) = D(f.1|_{V_x}) where f.1|_{V_x} is the
243	      image of f.1 under R = Γ(C.left, U) → Γ(C.left, V_x).
244	   c. IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen on (V_x, f.1|_{V_x})
245	      gives IsLocalization.Away (f.1|_{V_x}) Γ(C.left, V_x ⊓ D(f.1)).
246	   d. instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid
247	      upgrades to IsLocalizedModule (powers f.1) of the algebra-induced
248	      LinearMap Γ(V_x) → Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f.1)).
249	2. IsLocalizedModule.pi packages these per-coord instances into
250	   IsLocalizedModule (powers f.1) of LinearMap.pi(per-coord-restriction),
251	   for free as a typeclass instance.
252	3. Identify this LinearMap.pi with the slice-cover Čech differential
253	   at degree n by extensionality (LinearMap.pi_ext' is one option).
254	4. Use IsLocalizedModule.iso + LinearEquiv.exact_iff to transport
255	   h_a₀_fun f (slice-cover exactness) to exactness of the localised
256	   K₀ differential.
257	```
258	
259	**LOC**: ~80-120 LOC of glue. Strategy-critic's ~80 LOC estimate is
260	valid at the low end of this range — bridging step 1d (per-coord
261	algebra + IsLocalization.Away + adapter) is the bulk of the work.
262	
263	### For Q2 (`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` sorry at BasicOpenCech.lean:1120)
264	
265	**MATHLIB_GAP_CONFIRMED via the project's choice of `ModuleCat k`**. Two
266	viable paths:
267	
268	**Path B (tactical, ~30-50 LOC, RECOMMENDED FIRST)**: at the very top
269	of `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`'s body — **before** the
270	`dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, …]` at L998 — `set` the named family:
271	
272	```lean
273	let F := cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n
274	```
275	
276	then after the dsimp+simp unfolds at L998-L1007, attempt a single
277	`change`-pivot rewriting the goal's `∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing_anonymous_i ≫ eqToHom`
278	into `∑ i, (-1)^↑i • F i ≫ eqToHom`. Because `F` was defined as the
279	named extraction of that same `Pi.lift_thing`, the equality is by
280	`rfl` at the elaborator level — provided the dsimp+simp chain hasn't
281	whnf-reduced past the named family.
282	
283	If the `change` succeeds, `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` applies with
284	`G := fun i ↦ F i ≫ eqToHom`-binder form and per-summand R-linearity
285	from `cechCofaceMap_summand_family_R_linear` becomes a direct
286	binder-level invocation.
287	
288	**Critical experiment**: have iter-107+'s prover run `lean_multi_attempt`
289	with `["set F := cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n", "change ∑ i, ...", "exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux ..."]`
290	at L998 (BEFORE the dsimp) and at L1014 (AFTER the dsimp+simp+show
291	chain). One of the two positions should land a defeq for the
292	`change`. If neither does, fall back to Path A.
293	
294	**Path A (architectural, ~150-250 LOC, FALLBACK)**: refactor the
295	local-to-global proof to operate on a `ModuleCat R`-valued Čech
296	complex.
297	
298	```
299	1. Define toModuleKPresheaf_R : (Opens.subType (≤ U))ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat R
300	   where R := Γ(C.left, U). The presheaf restricts toModuleKPresheaf
301	   to opens contained in U, with each section module the (R-module
302	   image of the) original Γ(C.left, V) under the restriction
303	   R-algebra structure.
304	
305	2. Define K₀_R := cechCochain_R := (cechComplexFunctor with A :=
306	   ModuleCat R) on the basic-open cover restricted to ≤ U.
307	
308	3. Identify K₀ = (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (algebraMap k R)).mapHomologicalComplex K₀_R
309	   (R is a k-algebra via the structure morphism k → Γ(C.left, ⊤) →
310	   Γ(C.left, U) = R; the restriction-scalars functor is k-linear).
311	
312	4. R-linearity of K₀.f is INTRINSIC to K₀_R.f's type (it's a
313	   morphism in ModuleCat R, so R-linear by definition). The
314	   restrictScalars functor preserves linearity, so K₀.f (which IS
315	   K₀_R.f with k-restricted view) is R-linear when viewed through
316	   the R-module structure transported back to K₀.X_i via the
317	   forgetful.
318	
319	5. The iter-099+ alternating-sum infrastructure
320	   (alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux, etc.) becomes redundant: it was
321	   only needed because R-linearity was being certified post-hoc.
322	
323	6. cechCofaceMap_pi_smul and h_loc_exact both close via the
324	   restrictScalars + IsLocalizedModule.pi route directly on K₀_R,
325	   without any post-hoc pattern-matching.
326	```
327	
328	LOC: ~150-250. Eliminates the 7-iter-stuck pattern but is a deep
329	restructuring.
330	
331	**Recommended sequencing for iter-107+**:
332	- iter-107 prover: attempt Path B (tactical `change`) with the
333	  `lean_multi_attempt` probe described above. Time-box at 1 iter.
334	- iter-108 (if Path B fails): plan agent dispatches a refactor subagent
335	  to execute Path A on a feature branch.
336	
337	## Caveats
338	
339	- **Path B depends on a `rfl`-defeq** between the post-unfold goal
340	  shape and the named family. The iter-099 → iter-103 prover history
341	  documents multiple positions in the proof where Lean's whnf reducer
342	  destroys the named-family head symbol; if `set F` is shadowed before
343	  the dsimp/simp chain bakes the unfolds into the goal, the `change`
344	  will fail. The probe should test BOTH "set BEFORE dsimp" and "set
345	  AFTER dsimp" positions.
346	
347	- **Path A requires re-doing the `e_i` / `h_mod_pi_i` letI scaffolding**
348	  inside `h_K₀_exact` — the current scaffolding (BasicOpenCech.lean:
349	  1605-1694) builds R-module structures by hand on `∀ i, Z_i i`-types.
350	  Under Path A these instances become free: the morphism in `ModuleCat R`
351	  already carries the R-module structure. The refactor would simplify
352	  the proof considerably *if* the bridge to `ModuleCat k` via
353	  restrictScalars composes cleanly with the project's existing
354	  `cechCochain` / `cechComplexFunctor` API.
355	
356	- **Both paths preserve the strategy-critic's broader plan**: Q1's
357	  recipe (`IsLocalizedModule.pi` + slice-cover identification +
358	  `LinearEquiv.exact_iff`) is independent of which Q2 path closes
359	  `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`. Path A removes the need for `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`
360	  entirely; Path B closes it directly.
361	
362	- **Mathlib has the building blocks for both Q1 and Q2 Path A**:
363	  - `Mathlib.RingTheory.TensorProduct.IsBaseChangePi:93` —
364	    `IsLocalizedModule.pi`.
365	  - `Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.IsLocalization` —
366	    `instIsLocalizedModuleToLinearMapToAlgHomOfIsLocalizationAlgebraMapSubmonoid`.
367	  - `Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LocalizedModule.Basic` —
368	    `IsLocalizedModule.iso`, `IsLocalizedModule.linearMap_ext`.
369	  - `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme:716` —
370	    `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`.
371	  - `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.RestrictScalars` (and
372	    `.../ChangeOfRings.lean`) — the `restrictScalars` functor for
373	    Path A.
374	  - `Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Exactness:211` —
375	    `exact_of_localized_span`, already the closer in the project's
376	    L1786 step.
377	
378	- **Both paths share the same `exact_of_localized_span` consumer at
379	  L1786**; they differ only in HOW `h_loc_exact` is produced and HOW
380	  R-linearity of `f_R`/`g_R` is established.
381	
382	## Related analogies
383	- [[cech-koszul-precedent]] — iter-059 audit of Čech /
384	  extra-degeneracy / `exact_of_isLocalized_span` precedents. The
385	  present analogy refines that file's "Step 2 localized identification
386	  (~60-100 LOC)" estimate with the concrete `IsLocalizedModule.pi`
387	  call and confirms the per-coord adapter chain (`IsLocalization.Away`
388	  → `instIsLocalizedModule...AlgebraMapSubmonoid` → `IsLocalizedModule.pi`).
389	
390	---
391	*Iteration: 106*

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechToCohomology
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.QcohTildeSections
8	
9	/-!
10	# Serre vanishing on affines (Stacks 02KG) — affine cover-system infrastructure
11	
12	Project-local: builds the *affine cover system* feeding the basis-comparison criterion
13	`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (01EO). The basis `B` is the distinguished opens `D(f)`
14	of an affine scheme, and the admissible coverings `Cov` are the standard open covers.
15	
16	This file builds the cover-system infrastructure (Lane 1 of the 02KG decomposition) as far
17	as possible; the quasi-coherent seed `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh` and the top
18	`affine_serre_vanishing` are handed off to the assembly iteration (they consume Lane 2's
19	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`).
20	-/
21	
22	universe u
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	-- Re-activate the (file-local) `HasExt` instance from `AbsoluteCohomology.lean` so that the
29	-- `Ext`-based absolute cohomology resolves here without the slow `HasSmallLocalizedHom` search.
30	attribute [local instance] hasExtModules
31	
32	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
33	
34	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — distinguished opens closed under finite intersection -/
35	
36	/-- **Distinguished opens are closed under finite intersection** (Stacks 02KG, condition (1)).
37	For a family `s : ι → R` of ring elements and a Čech multi-index `σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι`, the
38	`(p + 1)`-fold intersection of distinguished opens `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k})` is again a distinguished
39	open, namely `D(∏ₖ s_{σ k})`; hence it lies in the basis of distinguished opens. This discharges
40	the `faces_mem` field of the affine cover system. Project-local: re-export of `basicOpen_sprod`
41	in the membership shape the `BasisCovSystem.faces_mem` field consumes. -/
42	theorem affine_faces_mem {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} (s : ι → R)
43	    {p : ℕ} (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
44	    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
45	      ∈ Set.range (fun f : R => (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens)) :=
46	  ⟨∏ k, s (σ k), (basicOpen_sprod (p + 1) s σ).symm⟩
47	
48	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — covering-datum bridge to the open-cover Čech form -/
49	
50	/-- **The standard affine open cover realizes the distinguished-open family.** For a spanning
51	family `s : ι → R`, the `i`-th member of the standard affine open cover
52	`affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs` of `Spec R` has open range exactly the distinguished open
53	`D(s_i)`, i.e. `coverOpen 𝒰 i = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)`. This is the open-level half of the
54	covering-datum bridge (`lem:cover_datum_bridge`): funext-rewriting the raw family `c.2 = fun i ↦
55	D(s_i)` to `coverOpen 𝒰` identifies the two section Čech complexes, so the Čech cohomology computed
56	over the raw `CovDatum` and over the `X.OpenCover` agree. Project-local: needed to feed the
57	`X.OpenCover`-shaped `injective_cech_acyclic` into the raw-family `BasisCovSystem` fields. -/
58	theorem coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} [Finite ι]
59	    (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (i : ι) :
60	    coverOpen (Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs)) i
61	      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i) := by
62	  unfold coverOpen
63	  change (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (s i))))).opensRange = _
64	  apply TopologicalSpace.Opens.ext
65	  change Set.range (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (s i))))).base = _
66	  rw [Spec.map_base]
67	  exact PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_comap_range (Localization.Away (s i)) (s i)
68	
69	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — injective acyclicity for the standard affine cover -/
70	
71	/-- **Injective Čech-acyclicity for the standard affine cover** (Stacks 02KG, `injective_acyclic`
72	field; Stacks `lemma-injective-trivial-cech`). For a spanning family `s : ι → R` (so the
73	distinguished opens `D(s_i)` cover `Spec R`) and an injective `O_X`-module `I`, the positive-degree
74	section Čech cohomology over the standard cover vanishes:
75	`Ȟ^q(𝒰, I) = 0` for all `q > 0`. Reduces the raw-family `cechCohomology` to the `X.OpenCover` form
76	via `coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan` and applies `injective_cech_acyclic`. Project-local: this is
77	the affine instantiation of `injective_cech_acyclic` discharging the
78	`BasisCovSystem.injective_acyclic` field for standard covers of the whole affine. -/
79	theorem affine_injective_acyclic {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} [Finite ι]
80	    (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
81	    (I : (Spec R).Modules) [Injective I] (q : ℕ) (hq : 0 < q) :
82	    IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
83	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj I) q) := by
84	  have hbridge : (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
85	      = coverOpen (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover := by
86	    funext i; exact (coverOpen_affineOpenCoverOfSpan s hs i).symm
87	  haveI : Finite (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover.I₀ :=
88	    inferInstanceAs (Finite ι)
89	  rw [cechCohomology, hbridge]
90	  exact injective_cech_acyclic (Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop s hs).openCover I q hq
91	
92	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves colimits
93	
94	The gap-fill `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits` / `toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms` (blueprint
95	`lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`, `lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`) — the statement that
96	`SheafOfModules.toSheaf R` (the forgetful functor to abelian sheaves) preserves finite colimits,
97	hence epimorphisms — is the foundational ingredient for `affine_surj_of_vanishing` (and hence for
98	the `surj_of_vanishing` field of `affineCoverSystem`).  Mathlib provides only the limit-side dual
99	`PreservesFiniteLimits (toSheaf R)` (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Limits`); the
100	colimit side is genuinely absent because epis of sheaves of modules are only *locally* surjective
101	and no factorisation through the right adjoint `forget` can capture it.
102	
103	The build below mirrors Mathlib's `…/ModuleCat/Sheaf/Colimits.lean` construction but routes through
104	the **sheafification square** `PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf (𝟙 R.obj)` (not through
105	`forget`).  Write `L := PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj)`, a left adjoint (counit iso).
106	Step 1: `L ⋙ toSheaf R ≅ toPresheaf R.obj ⋙ presheafToSheaf J Ab` (the square), and the right-hand
107	composite preserves finite colimits (`toPresheaf` does objectwise; `presheafToSheaf` is a left
108	adjoint).  Step 2 (descent): for a finite diagram `F`, `F ≅ (F ⋙ forget R) ⋙ L`; applying the
109	colimit-preserving `L` and then `L ⋙ toSheaf R` to a colimit cocone of `F ⋙ forget R` exhibits
110	`toSheaf R` as preserving the colimit of `F`.  Epi-preservation follows from `WalkingSpan` being
111	finite. -/
112	
113	/-- **The underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves finite colimits** (blueprint
114	`lem:toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`).  The forgetful functor `SheafOfModules.toSheaf R` from
115	sheaves of `R`-modules to the underlying sheaves of abelian groups preserves finite colimits — the
116	right-exact dual of the Mathlib-supplied `PreservesFiniteLimits (toSheaf R)`.  Project-local: the
117	missing colimit side, built through the sheafification square (never through the right adjoint
118	`forget`). -/
119	theorem toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits.{v', u', w'} {C : Type u'} [Category.{w'} C]
120	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} (R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v'})
121	    [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] :
122	    PreservesFiniteColimits (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R) := by
123	  have step1 : PreservesFiniteColimits
124	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj) ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R) :=
125	    preservesFiniteColimits_of_natIso
126	      (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationCompToSheaf (𝟙 R.obj)).symm
127	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfSize (PresheafOfModules.sheafification.{v'} (𝟙 R.obj)) :=
128	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)).leftAdjoint_preservesColimits
129	  constructor
130	  intro K _ _
131	  constructor
132	  intro F
133	  set D := F ⋙ SheafOfModules.forget R with hD
134	  have e : F ≅ D ⋙ PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj) :=
135	    Functor.isoWhiskerLeft F
136	      (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 R.obj)).counit).symm
137	  have hc : IsColimit (colimit.cocone D) := colimit.isColimit D
138	  have hLc := isColimitOfPreserves (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj)) hc
139	  have hF := isColimitOfPreserves
140	    (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj) ⋙ SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) hc
141	  haveI hpres : PreservesColimit (D ⋙ PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 R.obj))
142	      (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) :=
143	    preservesColimit_of_preserves_colimit_cocone hLc hF
144	  exact preservesColimit_of_iso_diagram (SheafOfModules.toSheaf R) e.symm
145	
146	/-- **The underlying-abelian-sheaf functor preserves epimorphisms** (blueprint
147	`lem:to_sheaf_preserves_epi`).  A one-line corollary of `toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits`:
148	finite-colimit preservation implies preservation of pushouts (`WalkingSpan`), hence of
149	epimorphisms.  This is the instance unlocking the passage from a module epimorphism to local
150	surjectivity in `affine_surj_of_vanishing`. -/
151	theorem toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{v', u', w'} {C : Type u'} [Category.{w'} C]
152	    {J : GrothendieckTopology C} (R : Sheaf J RingCat.{v'})
153	    [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v'}] :
154	    (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R).PreservesEpimorphisms := by
155	  haveI : PreservesColimitsOfShape WalkingSpan (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v'} R) :=
156	    (toSheaf_preservesFiniteColimits R).preservesFiniteColimits WalkingSpan
157	  exact preservesEpimorphisms_of_preservesColimitsOfShape _
158	
159	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — standard covers are cofinal -/
160	
161	/-- **Standard covers are cofinal among open covers of a distinguished open** (Stacks 02KG, Tag
162	009L; blueprint `lem:standard_cover_cofinal`).  Given a distinguished open `D(f)` of `Spec R` and an
163	arbitrary open cover `W : α → (Spec R).Opens` of it, there is a finite standard subcover refining
164	it: finitely many `g : Fin n → R` with `D(f) = ⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` and each `D(gᵢ) ≤ W (φ i)`.  This is the
165	refinement step invoked in `affine_surj_of_vanishing`.  Project-local: combines quasi-compactness of
166	`D(f)` with the basic-open basis. -/
167	theorem standard_cover_cofinal {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R) {α : Type u}
168	    (W : α → (Spec R).Opens)
169	    (hcov : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ ⨆ a, W a) :
170	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → α),
171	      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f : (Spec R).Opens) = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ∧
172	      ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i) := by
173	  classical
174	  set Uf : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f with hUf
175	  have hK : IsCompact (Uf : Set ↥(Spec R)) := PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen f
176	  have hbasis := PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens (R := R)
177	  -- `B r` is the distinguished open `D(r)` typed as an open of `Spec R`.
178	  let B : R → (Spec R).Opens := fun r => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
179	  -- Index type: basic opens contained in `Uf ⊓ W a`, carrying the witness `a`.
180	  let I := {p : R × α // B p.1 ≤ Uf ⊓ W p.2}
181	  let cover : I → Set ↥(Spec R) := fun p => (B p.1.1 : Set ↥(Spec R))
182	  have hopen : ∀ i, IsOpen (cover i) := fun i => (B i.1.1).isOpen
183	  -- The chosen basic opens cover `Uf`.
184	  have hsub : (Uf : Set ↥(Spec R)) ⊆ ⋃ i, cover i := by
185	    intro x hx
186	    have hxUf : x ∈ Uf := hx
187	    obtain ⟨a, hxa⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov hxUf)
188	    have hxinf : x ∈ ((Uf ⊓ W a : (Spec R).Opens) : Set ↥(Spec R)) := by
189	      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf]
190	      exact ⟨hxUf, hxa⟩
191	    obtain ⟨v, hvrange, hxv, hvsub⟩ :=
192	      hbasis.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxinf (Uf ⊓ W a).isOpen
193	    obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := hvrange
194	    have hr' : (B r : Set ↥(Spec R)) = v := hr
195	    have hle : B r ≤ Uf ⊓ W a := by
196	      rw [← SetLike.coe_subset_coe, hr']; exact hvsub
197	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨(r, a), hle⟩, ?_⟩
198	    change x ∈ (B r : Set ↥(Spec R))
199	    rw [hr']; exact hxv
200	  -- Quasi-compactness: extract a finite subcover.
